bestPractices.why: Generic, delayed feedback doesn't change behavior; specific next-actions ('add 35g protein at lunch') delivered before the relevant meal do, while consolidating submissions keeps the coach scalable.
bestPractices.exampleApp: HubFit; Everfit; Practice Better

bestPractices.practice: Present meal plans as flexible weekly cards with one-tap, macro-matched swaps and equivalence groups (allergy/dislike-aware), modeled on Exchange Lists
bestPractices.why: Reduces decision fatigue (the thing that keeps users in planners) and lets clients personalize without breaking the macro target or the coach's intent.
bestPractices.exampleApp: PlanEat AI; Eat This Much; Prospre

bestPractices.practice: Meet WCAG 2.2 AA explicitly in both themes: ≥4.5:1 text contrast, ≥44/48px touch targets, Dynamic Type, screen-reader labels on icons; never rely on color alone for macro ring segments
bestPractices.why: Dark mode is now expected (>80% of users) but naive inversion fails contrast; macro colors are meaningless to color-blind users without text labels. Apple/EAA/ADA Title II make this increasingly non-optional.
bestPractices.exampleApp: Apple App Store Connect dark-interface criteria; W3C WAI

bestPractices.practice: Mobile-first the logging path: minimize taps to log, keep primary actions in thumb reach, use dvh not vh, and make 'resume after a lapse' as frictionless as first-time logging
bestPractices.why: Adherence decays steeply (5.4→1.4 days/week by week 12); the product must make re-engagement trivial and never gate it behind guilt or setup friction.
bestPractices.exampleApp: MyFitnessPal decay data (PLOS/Fitia); general 2026 mobile-first guidance

notableApps.name: MacroFactor
notableApps.whatTheyDoWell: Defined the 'adherence-neutral' standard: no streaks, no red over-target numbers, no good/bad food labels, no shame pop-ups. Dynamic TDEE/macro adjustments based on actual intake + weight change rather than how well you obeyed. Colorful but non-judgmental dashboard; widgets. Widely credited with a healthier relationship with food and more complete logging.

notableApps.name: Nutrola
notableApps.whatTheyDoWell: Combines photo + voice + barcode + text in one meal; reports ~3x higher logging rate vs manual-entry average and ~8.4% MAPE on AI estimates. Strong home-screen widget system (calorie ring + macro bars + water + quick-log).

notableApps.name: HubFit / Everfit / Practice Better
notableApps.whatTheyDoWell: Unified review inbox; schedulable daily/weekly/monthly check-ins; side-by-side progress photo/measurement comparison; async Loom video feedback; timely-specific-adaptive coaching loop with automated data collection.

notableApps.name: PlanEat AI / Eat This Much / Prospre
notableApps.whatTheyDoWell: Flexible weekly plans, repeatable meals, real-world grocery lists, and one-tap macro-matched meal swaps with allergy/dislike-aware substitutions and user-defined swap rules (digital Exchange-List equivalences).

notableApps.name: Fitia / Cal AI / PlateLens
notableApps.whatTheyDoWell: Photo-first logging with confidence display and easy correction; PlateLens holds the lowest validated per-meal error (~13% MAE). Demonstrate the confirm/correct workflow that pairs AI speed with manual accuracy.

researchFindings.topic: How coaching/PT platforms handle nutrition modules (June 2026) — the competitive bar EVA must clear to sell nutrition to coaches and let real nutritionists work

keyFindings.title: Trainerize: native basic tracking + paid Smart Meal Planner add-on + MyFitnessPal sync
keyFindings.detail: Basic nutrition (USDA-verified food DB, barcode scan, calorie+macro targets, compliance scores) is included on all paid plans and built in-house. Advanced meal planning is the 'Smart Meal Planner' add-on at USD 20-45/mo (free on Studio Plus), generating up to 7 days of meals from 1,000+ recipes by macro target + dietary preference, with swaps and grocery lists. MyFitnessPal Premium macros/calories now appear as widgets inside the Trainerize Nutrition Center. White-label app branding is a USD 169 one-time fee + Apple dev fee, but branding does NOT extend to meal-plan outputs — no branded meal-plan PDF, limited allergy filtering, so it falls short for RDs selling meal plans as a service.

keyFindings.title: TrueCoach: thin nutrition — relies on MyFitnessPal integration, no real meal planning
keyFindings.detail: Built-in nutrition is deliberately limited: macros+calories pull in from MyFitnessPal on Standard (USD 57.99/mo) and Pro plans — food-logging visibility, NOT meal planning. Missing entirely: recipe database, AI meal-plan generation from macro targets, allergy/intolerance filtering, per-client dietary customization, white-label meal-plan outputs, client-facing nutrition portal. TrueCoach's marketed 'AI Meal Plan Generator' is actually a free downloadable Notion template (100+ recipes), not integrated into the product. Coaches needing meal plans must bolt on a dedicated tool.

keyFindings.title: Everfit: in-house macro tracking + AI food scanner; meal plans are a separate add-on
keyFindings.detail: Basic macro tracking (Food Journal / Macros) is included on Pro and Studio; clients log manually or sync MyFitnessPal AND Cronometer. Full meal planning (Meal Plan Generator from goals/preferences/restrictions + recipe books) is a separate USD 33/mo add-on. 2026 differentiator: MacroSnap, an AI-powered food-photo scanner built inside Everfit (no searching/typing/manual macro entry) — lowering the logging-friction bar.

keyFindings.title: FitBudd: all-in-one white-label app with in-house nutrition bundled
keyFindings.detail: Positioned as a fully white-labeled (iOS + Android, app-store listings, logo/colors, Apple/Google Pay) all-in-one. Nutrition (meal planning, macro tracking, 1M+ food library, barcode scanner inside the branded app) is built in and bundled rather than charged as a separate nutrition add-on. Strong on branding breadth; the differentiator is the per-coach branded native app, not nutrition depth.

keyFindings.title: Kahunas: in-house nutrition with large verified DB, macro-by-meal, integrated check-ins
keyFindings.detail: Built-in nutrition: 100% verified DB of 1.4M+ foods, 3,000 restaurant items, 600,000+ barcodes. Fully personalized meal plans with specific foods, portions, meal timing; macro breakdown BY MEAL; in-app food logging; supplement and cardio plans. Tight coupling of nutrition + training + progress photos + messaging in one portal, with automated, customizable check-in reminders and tracked weekly check-in history. Strong on the check-in-as-glue model.

keyFindings.title: PT Distinction: deepest MyFitnessPal integration + habit coaching + assignable nutrition habits
keyFindings.detail: Claims the most complete MyFitnessPal integration in the industry — food, meals, macros, calories AND micronutrients flow in, with daily/weekly summaries vs goals. AI-assisted meal-plan example generation, hundreds of example plans. Standout: HABIT coaching — build a nutrition habit once, assign to many clients with automated tracking + check-ins; reminders cited to lift adherence 90%+. Nutrition is woven into scheduling/automation rather than sold as a separate macro add-on.

keyFindings.title: My PT Hub: nutrition bundled at no extra cost, but explicitly not a meal-planning specialist
keyFindings.detail: Per-client macro/calorie targets via built-in calculator; 650,000-item food DB with barcode scan + MyFitnessPal sync; unlimited custom nutrition plans assignable — all INCLUDED in subscription, no add-on. White-label app with store listings, custom logo/splash/colors/icon. Explicit limits: no curated recipe library, no allergy filter, no automated plan generation — 'serviceable for basic macro targets and meal logging, not serious meal planning.' Many coaches pair it with Promealplan for the plans.

keyFindings.title: Industry split: PT platforms do tracking/macros in-house but outsource serious meal planning
keyFindings.detail: Across the category the pattern is consistent: coaching platforms build (or integrate via MyFitnessPal/Cronometer) calorie+macro tracking natively, then either (a) charge a meal-plan add-on (Trainerize 20-45/mo, Everfit 33/mo) or (b) bundle a shallow planner (My PT Hub, FitBudd). NONE clear the bar for registered dietitians on meal planning: missing pieces are 200+ allergy/intolerance filters, dietitian-validated recipe libraries, automated macro-precise plan generation, food-exchange systems, and white-label branded meal-plan PDF/document outputs. Coaches who sell meal plans as a deliverable routinely bolt on a dedicated tool (Promealplan, That Clean Life) alongside the coaching app.

keyFindings.title: MyFitnessPal/Cronometer integration is table-stakes, not a moat
keyFindings.detail: Every serious platform (Trainerize, TrueCoach, Everfit, PT Distinction, My PT Hub) offers MyFitnessPal sync so clients don't double-log; Everfit and several others add Cronometer. The frontier is moving past sync: AI photo-based logging (Everfit MacroSnap), micronutrient pass-through (PT Distinction), and habit-based automation are the 2026 differentiators rather than the integration itself.

bestPractices.practice: Ship native calorie+macro tracking with a large verified food DB and barcode scan as INCLUDED baseline, not an add-on
bestPractices.why: My PT Hub, FitBudd, Kahunas and Trainerize all treat basic tracking as table-stakes bundled value (650k-1.4M food items + barcodes). EVA must match this floor or coaches won't consider it credible for nutrition at all.
bestPractices.exampleApp: Kahunas (1.4M foods, 600k barcodes); My PT Hub (650k, included)

bestPractices.practice: Offer MyFitnessPal (and ideally Cronometer) sync so clients don't double-log
bestPractices.why: It is universal across the category and removes the #1 client-adoption friction; absence reads as a missing feature. Pass through macros AND micronutrients, not just calories, to differentiate.
bestPractices.exampleApp: PT Distinction (full macro+micro pass-through); Everfit (MFP + Cronometer)

bestPractices.practice: Gate advanced meal-plan generation as a paid add-on, keep tracking free — the proven monetization split
bestPractices.why: Trainerize (Smart Meal Planner 20-45/mo) and Everfit (Meal Plans 33/mo) monetize meal planning separately while including tracking. This matches EVA's coach_addons self-service model (~CLP 9,990/module) and validates charging for the nutrition module specifically.
bestPractices.exampleApp: Trainerize Smart Meal Planner; Everfit Meal Plans & Recipe Books

bestPractices.practice: Make the check-in the glue that links nutrition + training + photos + messaging in one portal
bestPractices.why: Kahunas and PT Distinction win retention by tying weekly check-ins (with tracked history and automated reminders) to both nutrition adherence and training. Reminders are cited to lift adherence 90%+. EVA already has check_ins — linking nutrition compliance into it is high-leverage.
bestPractices.exampleApp: PT Distinction (automated habit check-ins); Kahunas (weekly check-in history in-app)

bestPractices.practice: Add habit/adherence coaching, not just numeric macro targets
bestPractices.why: PT Distinction's assignable nutrition habits (build once, assign to many, auto-track) are positioned as the best long-term-results driver and scale a coach's roster. Pure macro numbers are commoditized.
bestPractices.exampleApp: PT Distinction habit coaching

bestPractices.practice: To let REAL nutritionists work, clear the dietitian bar the PT apps all miss: allergy/intolerance filters, validated recipe library, automated macro-precise plan generation, food-exchange systems, and white-label branded meal-plan PDFs/documents
bestPractices.why: Every PT platform fails here (Trainerize/My PT Hub explicitly lack allergy filters and branded plan outputs), forcing RDs to bolt on Promealplan/That Clean Life. If EVA's nutrition module delivers branded outputs + allergy filters + exchanges, it can serve BOTH coaches and nutritionists in one white-label app — a genuine wedge competitors leave open. EVA already has food-exchange (intercambios) groundwork, which is rare in this set.
bestPractices.exampleApp: Gap vs Trainerize/My PT Hub; bar set by Promealplan (200+ allergy filters, validated recipes, branded outputs)

bestPractices.practice: Extend white-label branding to the nutrition/meal-plan outputs, not just the app shell
bestPractices.why: Trainerize brands the app but NOT the meal-plan output — a named weakness for coaches selling plans as their own practice. EVA is white-label-first per coach; branding the actual meal plan document is a cheap, differentiating win.
bestPractices.exampleApp: Trainerize (branding stops at app shell) — the gap to beat

bestPractices.practice: Invest in low-friction logging (AI food-photo scan) as the 2026 frontier
bestPractices.why: Everfit's MacroSnap (AI photo → macros, no typing) is the current differentiator beyond MFP sync; logging friction is the top reason client nutrition tracking dies. This is where the category is heading in 2026.
bestPractices.exampleApp: Everfit MacroSnap

notableApps.name: Trainerize
notableApps.whatTheyDoWell: Native basic tracking bundled + monetized Smart Meal Planner add-on (20-45/mo) + MyFitnessPal widgets in a unified Nutrition Center. Proves the free-tracking / paid-meal-planning split.

notableApps.name: PT Distinction
notableApps.whatTheyDoWell: Deepest MyFitnessPal integration (macros + micros + summaries) and assignable HABIT coaching with automated check-ins — adherence and scale, not just numbers.

notableApps.name: Everfit
notableApps.whatTheyDoWell: In-house macro tracking + MFP & Cronometer sync, and 2026 AI food-photo scanner (MacroSnap) that removes logging friction. Meal plans as a 33/mo add-on.

notableApps.name: Kahunas
notableApps.whatTheyDoWell: Huge verified food DB (1.4M), macro-by-meal personalized plans, and tight check-in-as-glue model linking nutrition, training, photos and messaging.

notableApps.name: My PT Hub
notableApps.whatTheyDoWell: Bundles unlimited nutrition plans at no extra cost with white-label app; honest about NOT being a meal-planning specialist (no recipes/allergy/auto-gen) — illustrates the dietitian gap.

notableApps.name: FitBudd
notableApps.whatTheyDoWell: Fully branded native iOS/Android app per coach with nutrition (1M+ foods, barcode) bundled in — branding breadth as the wedge.

notableApps.name: TrueCoach
notableApps.whatTheyDoWell: Clean training-first product; nutrition is intentionally thin (MFP food-logging only), showing the floor EVA must exceed — and the bolt-on-tool pain coaches feel.

notableApps.name: Promealplan
notableApps.whatTheyDoWell: Sets the professional bar PT apps miss: 1,000+ dietitian-validated recipes, 200+ allergy filters, automated macro-precise generation, white-label branded outputs — what real nutritionists need.

roleFindings.verdict: A well-built macro+exchange coaching tool with strong daily-loop UX and a genuinely clean exchanges module — but structurally capped below clinical grade by a 4-macro data ceiling, two backend generations side-by-side, and no single source of truth for adherence or "saved" state.

topConcerns.title: 4-macro data ceiling is a hard clinical wall
topConcerns.detail: foods carries only kcal/P/C/F (INTEGER), with no fiber, sodium, sugar, fat subtypes, or micronutrients anywhere in the schema, goals, logging, alerts, or PDFs. This single schema decision propagates through every surface and makes any clinical case (hypertension, renal, diabetes, pregnancy, deficiency) impossible. It is the dominant structural limitation — every other nutritionist-grade feature depends on lifting it first.

topConcerns.title: No single source of truth for adherence
topConcerns.detail: Two divergent formulas (client-detail counts all meal logs with no day-of-week filter; dashboard filters via Santiago day-of-week) feed four incompatible definitions of 'adherence' shown simultaneously across ring, strip, KPI, and today badge — they can legitimately disagree for the same student. The UI ships apologetic InfoTooltips to paper over it. This erodes trust in the core metric the whole product sells.

topConcerns.title: Consumed macros conflate plan-adherence with real intake, unlabeled
topConcerns.detail: calculateConsumedMacrosWithCompletionFallback credits full planned macros on completion; there is no off-plan entry, >100% portion, or barcode. Rings read 100% while the client ate something else, and streak/heatmap reward marking-done — gameable and can reinforce dishonest self-report. The UI implies real macro tracking without ever labeling it as plan-adherence.

topConcerns.title: High-blast-radius mutations are transaction-less and partial-failure-prone
topConcerns.detail: propagateTemplateChanges loops clients serially with no Promise.all, no DB transaction, and swallows most errors; org bulk-assign always targets ALL active clients (UI implies a subset), unconditionally deactivates prior plans, and inserts macro-only plans with no transaction. At 300-client team scale a mid-loop failure leaves some clients mutated, some untouched, some with zero active plan.

topConcerns.title: Two backend generations and fragmented surfaces accrue compounding tech debt
topConcerns.detail: Legacy grams path (NutritionService) bypasses the read-only repository and hits Supabase directly (pillar violation), while the exemplary exchanges module is client-plan-only. Three template stores (two dead-end), two food-create paths, two food-browse UIs, a disconnected /coach/recipes surface, split-brain save semantics (atomic vs 700ms debounce vs cycles action), and 9 nutrition entry points in coach review. No unified plan abstraction, gate resolver, or saved-state signal.

topConcerns.title: Mutable food rows rewrite history; positional reconciliation mis-attaches logs
topConcerns.detail: Macros read live from foods at calc time with no per-log nutrient snapshot, so editing a food silently changes every past log and makes analytics non-reproducible. Grams-mode meal reconciliation keys on order_index, so reorder+save can attach a client's historical logs to a different meal — the exchanges path was explicitly fixed to match by id; grams was not.

topConcerns.title: Scope columns are UPDATE-grantable to authenticated on nutrition_plans and foods
topConcerns.detail: client_id/coach_id/org_id on nutrition_plans, and the whole foods row, are writable by authenticated with only RLS WITH CHECK as a guard — contrary to the clients-hardening doctrine in CLAUDE.md. foods has no column allowlist, so a coach could flip coach_id→NULL to push a private food toward the global pool. Cross-tenant re-scope risk.

topConcerns.title: Empty-meal data corruption is unaddressed and unmonitored
topConcerns.detail: A 2026-04-30 manual audit found 17 empty meals across 6 clients and 2 clients with entirely empty plans still accruing daily logs. Nothing prevents saving an empty meal or activating an empty plan, these rows feed adherence math and export to PDF as valid, and the audit script is not in CI or any cron.

recommendations.title: Extend the food schema to a verified, source-tagged, micronutrient-capable model
recommendations.detail: Migrate foods to NUMERIC macros + a structured nutrient set (fiber, sodium, sugar, sat/unsat fat first; full vit/mineral panel as nullable cols or a food_nutrients sidecar). Add source (inta_safoods|fatsecret|openfoodfacts|usda|coach) + verified flag + external id, rank verified/local first. This foundational migration unblocks every clinical feature; do it before UI work. Region-layer the catalog: INTA/SAFOODS (manual ingest) + FatSecret API (Latam brands/barcodes) + Open Food Facts (free barcode) + USDA fallback.

recommendations.title: Collapse adherence to one pure tested function + label consumed-vs-tracked honestly
recommendations.detail: Extract a single computeAdherence(plan, logs, date|range) into lib/nutrition-utils (day-of-week-aware, one definition) consumed by client-detail, dashboard, ring, strip, and badge; delete the divergent formula. Label all 'consumed' macros explicitly as plan-adherence, not intake, until real intake logging exists. Remove the apologetic InfoTooltips once numbers reconcile.

recommendations.title: Make the exchanges module the reference arch; route grams writes through the repository
recommendations.detail: Give NutritionService a real write-capable nutrition.repository.ts (stop hitting Supabase directly), fix grams meal reconciliation to key on meal id (not order_index), and define a migration path so grams plans can become exchange-based. Target one plan abstraction with plan_mode as a presentation detail, not two backends. Allow exchange-based templates (today exchanges are client-plan-only).

recommendations.title: Move high-blast-radius mutations into transactional RPCs
recommendations.detail: Reimplement propagateTemplateChanges and org/team bulk-assign as SECURITY DEFINER RPCs (or service-role transactions) that are set-based, all-or-nothing, selective (honor the chosen subset), and error-surfacing. Add per-row error isolation + observability/alerting to the nutrition crons, batch+rate-limit the reminder cron, and skip students with nothing prescribed today.

recommendations.title: Apply the column-allowlist doctrine to nutrition tables + snapshot nutrients per log
recommendations.detail: REVOKE table UPDATE + GRANT UPDATE(allowlist) on nutrition_plans and foods, removing client_id/coach_id/org_id from authenticated writes (mirror the clients hardening already mandated in CLAUDE.md). Snapshot per-log nutrient values (not just plan-level targets) so food edits stop rewriting history. Add the food kcal cross-check (4P+4C+9F ≈ kcal) and a protein-floor/kcal-upper-bound safety rail server-side.

recommendations.title: Add DB-level integrity invariants for plans/meals
recommendations.detail: CHECK/trigger constraints (or service-layer guards backed by a scheduled CI check) preventing activation of empty plans and saving empty meals; reconcile the 17 known empty meals and 2 empty active plans. Promote the ad-hoc audit script into a scheduled integrity check with alerting.

recommendations.title: Consolidate nutrition into one canonical surface with unified saved-state
recommendations.detail: Pick one food-create path, one food-browse UI, and one template store (kill or merge org_nutrition_templates/team-pool dead-ends). Unify dirty/saved state across grams/exchanges/cycles into a single 'is this plan saved' signal. Fold the disconnected /coach/recipes Edamam surface into the builder's food search. Reduce the 9 coach-review nutrition entry points to one canonical nutrition review with adherence that reconciles.

recommendations.title: Wire body-composition into target-setting and unify the module gate
recommendations.detail: Replace the two parallel gates (tier canUseNutrition inline in RSC vs enabled_modules/coach_addons via assertModule) with one resolver every surface must call. Let the target engine consume ISAK/BIA lean-mass from the body_composition add-on (Mifflin default → Cunningham/Katch-McArdle when LBM present) instead of free-text daily_calories. Add structured allergy/intolerance enforcement so client_food_preferences can block allergens, not just pin favorites.

quickWins: Extract adherence into one shared pure function and delete the divergent client-detail formula (resolves cross-screen disagreement).
Label all 'consumed' macros as plan-adherence (not real intake) in web + mobile until intake logging exists.
REVOKE table UPDATE + GRANT column-allowlist on nutrition_plans and foods (closes the cross-tenant re-scope hole; mirrors existing clients doctrine).
Add CHECK/guard preventing activation of empty plans and saving empty meals; clean up the 17 known empty rows.
Promote audit-nutrition-empty-meals.mjs from a manual script to a scheduled integrity cron with alerting.
Switch grams meal reconciliation from order_index to meal id (the exchanges path already does this) to stop mis-attaching historical logs on reorder.
Add the 4P+4C+9F ≈ kcal cross-check on food save and a kcal upper-bound to the existing <1200 lower rail.
Add per-row error isolation to the cycle-advance cron so one bad row stops aborting the whole tenant run.

roleFindings.verdict: A solid macro/exchange CRUD backend with one genuinely clean module (exchanges) bolted onto a direct-to-Supabase legacy path; correctness is undermined by a 4-macro data ceiling, two divergent adherence formulas, intake-vs-adherence conflation, and transaction-less bulk/propagation paths that corrupt or partially-mutate production data.

topConcerns.title: Two divergent adherence formulas with 4 incompatible definitions shown simultaneously
topConcerns.detail: client-detail.service counts done/total over ALL meal logs (no day-of-week filter) while dashboard.service + the 'today' % filter via nutritionMealAppliesOnIsoYmdInSantiago. The Overview ring, Nutrición strip, dashboard KPI, and today badge can legitimately disagree for the same student, and 'adherence' simultaneously means logged-≥1-meal / %-of-plan-meals / weekly-avg-% / today-%. No single source of truth — the data is untrustworthy to a coach making decisions.

topConcerns.title: Transaction-less, non-selective, destructive org bulk-assign
topConcerns.detail: assignOrgNutritionPlanTemplateToClientsAction always targets ALL active org clients (UI implies a subset), unconditionally deactivates prior plans and inserts macro-only plans (no meals) wiping coach customization, with no DB transaction. A mid-operation failure leaves clients with zero active plan. The rich org_nutrition_templates.meal_names is never propagated. This is live production money-customer data being silently corrupted.

topConcerns.title: Empty-meal / empty-active-plan production data corruption, unguarded
topConcerns.detail: The 2026-04-30 audit found 17 empty meals across 6 clients and 2 clients with entirely empty active plans still accruing daily logs. Nothing at the DB or service layer prevents saving an empty meal or activating an empty plan, and the audit is a manual one-off script, not CI/cron. These rows feed adherence math and export to PDF as valid plans.

topConcerns.title: Scope columns (client_id/coach_id/org_id) are UPDATE-grantable to authenticated on nutrition_plans and foods
topConcerns.detail: Cross-tenant re-scoping is gated only by RLS WITH CHECK, not by grant revocation — contrary to the clients-hardening doctrine in CLAUDE.md. foods has no column allowlist at all, so a coach could flip coach_id→NULL to promote a private food toward the global pool. A re-scope hole on money-customer data.

topConcerns.title: Consumed macros = plan-adherence, not real intake, but presented as tracking
topConcerns.detail: calculateConsumedMacrosWithCompletionFallback credits full planned macros on completion; rings can read 100% while the client ate something entirely different. Streak/heatmap reward marking-done, making the metric gameable and able to reinforce dishonest self-report, while the UI implies real macro tracking without labeling it plan-adherence.

topConcerns.title: Serial, transaction-less template propagation that partially mutates at scale
topConcerns.detail: propagateTemplateChanges loops clients sequentially with many awaited queries each, no Promise.all, no DB transaction, and swallows most insert/update errors. For a 300-client team this is thousands of serial round-trips; a mid-loop error leaves earlier clients mutated and later untouched, with no rollback and no surfaced failure.

topConcerns.title: 4-macro data ceiling caps the system below clinical-grade
topConcerns.detail: foods stores only kcal/P/C/F (INTEGER), with no fiber, sodium, sugar, saturated/unsaturated fat, or micronutrients anywhere in goals, logging, PDFs, or alerts. This propagates through every surface and makes hypertension/renal/diabetes/pregnancy/deficiency management impossible — the dominant structural limitation for the nutritionist audience.

topConcerns.title: Food edits retroactively rewrite history; grams reconciliation keys on position
topConcerns.detail: Macros read live from foods at calc time with only plan-level snapshots, so editing a food silently changes every past log's computed intake — adherence analytics are non-reproducible. Separately, grams-mode meal reconciliation matches by order_index, so reorder+save can attach a client's historical logs to a different meal (the exchanges path was explicitly fixed to match by id; grams was not).

recommendations.title: Extract one canonical adherence + consumed-macros service
recommendations.detail: Create services/nutrition/adherence.ts with a single pure computeNutritionAdherence(plan, logs, range, tz) that is day-of-week-aware via nutritionMealAppliesOnIsoYmdInSantiago, plus one consumed-macros function explicitly named planAdherenceMacros (not 'consumed'). Route dashboard, client-detail, Overview ring, Nutrición strip, today badge, and PDF through it. Delete the client-detail done/total variant. Add a vitest covering a plan where weekly avg, today %, and meal-count diverge to lock the definitions.

recommendations.title: Add micronutrient columns + source/verification provenance to foods
recommendations.detail: Additive forward-only migration: numeric(8,2) columns for fiber, sodium, sugar, saturated/unsaturated fat, plus a vitamins/minerals jsonb for the long tail; add source_db (inta_safoods|fatsecret|open_food_facts|usda|coach_custom) and is_verified. Backfill existing 343 rows. Widen the 4 macro columns INTEGER→numeric(8,2) in the same migration to kill rounding loss. Add GRANT UPDATE allowlist EXCLUDING scope columns. Unblocks every clinical surface downstream.

recommendations.title: Lock down scope columns on nutrition_plans and foods (grant hardening)
recommendations.detail: One migration mirroring 20260611120001_clients_scoping_grants: REVOKE UPDATE ON nutrition_plans FROM authenticated; GRANT UPDATE on the editable allowlist (daily_calories, protein_g, carbs_g, fats_g, plan_mode, …) excluding client_id/coach_id/org_id/template_id. Same for foods, excluding coach_id/org_id. Extend tests/separation/module-grants.sql to assert nutrition_plans/foods drift. Re-scoping becomes service-role-only.

recommendations.title: Make bulk-assign and propagation transactional and set-based
recommendations.detail: Replace the serial loops with SECURITY DEFINER service-role RPCs doing deactivate+insert in a single transaction with all-or-nothing semantics, using set-based statements (UPDATE … WHERE client_id = ANY($1), INSERT … SELECT) instead of per-client round-trips. Make org bulk-assign respect an explicit client_id[] from the UI rather than 'all active'. Propagate org_nutrition_templates.meal_names, or kill the dead table and consolidate on nutrition_plan_templates.

recommendations.title: Add DB-level integrity guards for empty meals/plans + scheduled check
recommendations.detail: Trigger or check preventing an is_active plan from having zero food_items across its meals, and preventing save of a meal with no items. Convert audit-nutrition-empty-meals.mjs into a cron with alerting (or a daily SQL advisor query) so drift is caught automatically. Backfill-clean the existing 17 empty meals / 2 empty active plans.

recommendations.title: Per-log macro snapshotting + id-based grams reconciliation
recommendations.detail: Snapshot computed macros into nutrition_meal_logs at log time (mirror the existing target_*_at_log pattern down to per-food/per-meal consumed) so editing foods no longer rewrites history and analytics become reproducible. Change grams-mode meal reconciliation to match by id (as the exchanges path already does) instead of order_index, eliminating the reorder→mis-attached-logs bug.

recommendations.title: Integrate a verified Latam food DB behind the existing food search
recommendations.detail: Wire FatSecret Platform API (best Latam fit: per-country brands/barcodes, free tier) + Open Food Facts barcode first-pass behind FoodSearchDrawer/FoodBrowser and mobile search, writing into foods with source_db/is_verified set and verified/local ranked first. Manually ingest INTA/SAFOODS for Chilean whole foods (no API). Backend prerequisite for barcode logging and citable plans. Reuse the Edamam adapter pattern already in /coach/recipes.

recommendations.title: Unify the two nutrition gates behind one resolver
recommendations.detail: Single canUseNutritionFor(actor, plan) helper resolving BOTH the tier canUseNutrition check and enabled_modules/coach_addons (assertModule), plus the exchanges cross-dependency, in one place. Every RSC and every mutation calls it; remove the inline RSC tier check and the 2-3 hand-enforced cross-dependency sites so a future surface can't check one gate and forget the other.

recommendations.title: Move the legacy grams writes through nutrition.repository.ts
recommendations.detail: Add write functions to nutrition.repository.ts and route NutritionService through them, restoring the _data → services → repository → Supabase flow the pillars mandate. Collapse the FormData action format onto JSON (one serializer). Resolve the fate of org_nutrition_templates and the team_id template pool (consolidate or delete) to remove the three-store ambiguity. Precondition for safely adding tx/snapshot logic above.

recommendations.title: Harden crons: batching, per-row isolation, alerting, server-side kcal cross-check
recommendations.detail: Wrap cycle/reminder per-row work in try/catch-continue; batch + rate-limit the reminder cron and guard on 'has prescription today'; emit structured logs + alert on failure instead of console. Separately add a server-side macro-consistency validator (4P+4C+9F ≈ kcal, protein floor, kcal upper bound) on plan save and in exchange mode, so prescribed portions can't silently miss the stated goal — the 5% mismatch banner is grams-only today.

quickWins: Grant-harden scope columns on nutrition_plans/foods (REVOKE UPDATE + column allowlist) — one additive migration, mirrors existing clients pattern, closes a cross-tenant re-scope hole.
Add a CHECK/trigger blocking activation of a plan with zero food_items and backfill-clean the known 17 empty meals / 2 empty active plans from the 2026-04-30 audit.
Widen foods macro columns INTEGER → numeric(8,2) to stop rounding loss (same migration as the micronutrient add).
Rename every 'consumed macros' UI/string to 'plan adherence' until real intake exists — removes the silent correctness lie with zero schema change.
Add a vitest asserting weekly-avg vs today-% vs meal-count adherence diverge, to lock definitions before the canonical-service refactor.
Add a 'has prescription for today' guard to the nutrition-reminder cron so it stops pinging students with nothing scheduled.

roleFindings.verdict: Strong daily-loop UX sitting on a fragmented component graph: nutrition has no canonical home, ships 4 incompatible adherence numbers across 9 entry points, and the review surface is a 1411-line mega-component — the FE layer is the bottleneck, not the data model.

topConcerns.title: Adherence shows 4 contradictory numbers from 2 formulas on screens viewed together
topConcerns.detail: client-detail.service counts done/total over ALL meal logs (no day-of-week filter) while dashboard.service + the today% filter via nutritionMealAppliesOnIsoYmdInSantiago. Result: the Overview ring, Nutricion strip, dashboard KPI, and today badge legitimately disagree for the same student, and the UI papers over it with apologetic InfoTooltips. From the FE lens this is a single-source-of-truth failure: the number is computed in 4 places with 4 definitions (logged>=1 meal / % of plan meals / weekly avg % / today %). It erodes trust in every nutrition surface and makes the component contracts unverifiable. There must be exactly one useNutritionAdherence selector consumed everywhere.

topConcerns.title: NutritionTabB5 is a 1411-line mega-component mixing edit + analytics + ~12 cards
topConcerns.detail: The single most-used coach review surface is one unmaintainable file that interleaves editing affordances, analytics cards, navigation-out links, and data fetching. It cannot be unit-tested, lazy-loaded, or reused on mobile, and every nutrition change risks regressing the whole tab. This is the FE root cause of the broader fragmentation: because no composable nutrition presentation primitives exist (AdherenceRing, MacroBars, MealAdherenceList, ConsumedVsTarget), nutrition got copy-pasted into 9 surfaces with divergent colors and math instead of composed from shared atoms/organisms.

topConcerns.title: Nutrition is scattered across 9 entry points with no canonical home and inconsistent visual language
topConcerns.detail: Coach review of one student bleeds nutrition into 4 of 6 tabs plus the dashboard: a hint string under Adherencia, a hidden 2nd sheet tab, an overloaded nav badge, a 3rd adherence ring, a red dot inside the TRAINING card, a top alert silently suppressed by a priority cascade, charts hidden under a body-composition pill, and the mega-tab. Macro colors differ between page (orange/blue/yellow) and dashboard (rose/amber/emerald). There is no design token set for macros and no shared layout deciding where nutrition lives. A coach doing a weekly review hops 5+ places to reconcile numbers that don't reconcile.

topConcerns.title: Split-brain persistence: no unified dirty/saved state for 'is this plan saved'
topConcerns.detail: Grams data saves atomically on Save; exchange portions/variants auto-save on a 700ms debounce; cycles via a third action. PlanBuilder has no single dirty-tracking/saved indicator, so the coach cannot reason about whether their work is persisted. This is a classic FE state-management gap (the project mandates explicit state patterns) and a direct cause of data-loss anxiety and the empty-plan corruption — nothing in the builder blocks saving/activating an empty meal or plan, and those feed adherence math and export to PDF as valid.

topConcerns.title: Duplicated nutrition logic across web/mobile/PDF with drift, plus dead-wired features
topConcerns.detail: Swap/macro-transform logic is duplicated across NutritionShell, NutritionDailySummary, FoodSearchDrawer vs FoodBrowser (two browse UIs), and two food-create paths (FoodSearchDrawer JSON vs AddFoodSheet FormData) with duplicated validation/rounding. Two live serialization formats (legacy FormData + JSON actions) coexist. On mobile, activeSwapMealIds is fetched but dead-wired (alumno cannot apply coach swaps) and the entire exchanges module is absent. expo-camera is installed with no barcode/photo affordance — the single biggest unused native capability and the #1 friction-reducer per the 2026 bar.

topConcerns.title: Consumed macros are plan-adherence mislabeled as intake; UX is subtly dishonest
topConcerns.detail: calculateConsumedMacrosWithCompletionFallback credits full planned macros on completion, so rings can read 100% while the client ate something else. Streak/heatmap reward marking-done, which is gameable and can reinforce dishonest self-report. The UI presents these as real macro tracking without labeling them as plan-adherence. Separately the streak/all-or-nothing framing runs against the 2026 adherence-neutral best practice (restartable consistency, no shame), which is a retention lever the FE controls directly.

recommendations.title: Extract one useNutritionAdherence selector + nutrition presentation primitives
recommendations.detail: Create a single pure selector (one day-of-week-aware definition, Santiago tz) consumed by every surface, and a set of composable organisms: AdherenceRing, MacroBars, MealAdherenceList, ConsumedVsTarget. Delete the per-surface recomputations in client-detail.service and the inline dashboard math. This kills the 4-number problem and is the prerequisite for de-duplicating the 9 entry points.

recommendations.title: Decompose NutritionTabB5 into edit vs review halves built from the shared primitives
recommendations.detail: Split the 1411-line component into a NutritionReview view (read-only, composed from AdherenceRing/MacroBars/MealAdherenceList, lazy-loaded, unit-testable) and a thin edit-launcher that links to PlanBuilder. Each card becomes an independently renderable organism reusable on the dashboard sheet and mobile. This collapses the fragmentation at its source.

recommendations.title: Define a canonical nutrition design-token set and a single review home
recommendations.detail: One macro color/label mapping (protein/carbs/fat) in the theme, applied web + dashboard + mobile + PDF; macro color must never be the only signal (WCAG 2.2 AA, pair with labels for color-blind users). Make the Nutricion tab the one canonical home; demote the scattered hint string, the training-card red dot, and the suppressed top-alert to consistent deep-links into it rather than parallel computations.

recommendations.title: Unify PlanBuilder persistence with a single dirty/saved state machine + empty-plan guard
recommendations.detail: Introduce one useReducer-driven save state ('saved' | 'dirty' | 'saving' | 'error') covering grams, exchanges, and cycles, with a visible saved indicator. Block save/activate of empty meals and empty plans client-side AND server-side. Add the 4P+4C+9F approx kcal cross-check as a non-blocking banner in both grams and exchanges modes (currently grams-only).

recommendations.title: Consolidate duplicated food/swap/transform code into one shared package
recommendations.detail: Collapse FoodSearchDrawer vs FoodBrowser into one food-browse component, the two food-create paths into one validated path, and move swap/macro-transform into the shared nutrition-utils consumed by web, mobile, and PDF. Pick JSON as the single serialization format and retire the legacy FormData actions. Wire activeSwapMealIds on mobile so alumno swaps actually work.

recommendations.title: Ship barcode + camera capture and label consumed numbers honestly
recommendations.detail: Use the already-installed expo-camera for barcode scan (fastest, most accurate capture per 2026 bar) and a photo-as-draft path; reconcile to one verified source-tagged DB. Relabel rings/cards as 'cumplimiento del plan' (plan adherence) vs real intake wherever portions are estimated, and reframe the streak as restartable 'X de 7 dias' consistency rather than an all-or-nothing chain.

quickWins: Unify macro colors across page (orange/blue/yellow) and dashboard (rose/amber/emerald) into one theme token mapping with text labels for color-blind users.
Add a 'cumplimiento del plan' label next to consumed-macro rings so the number isn't read as real intake.
Replace the all-or-nothing streak copy with restartable 'X de 7 dias' consistency framing (no red/shame states).
Pick one adherence definition for the nav badge so it stops being overloaded between '!' risk and meal count.
Delete the apologetic InfoTooltips once the single adherence selector lands — they exist only to excuse the divergence.
Add an empty-meal/empty-plan client-side guard in PlanBuilder so coaches can't save or activate empty plans that then export to PDF.

roleFindings.verdict: EVA's nutrition is a competent web-first PWA with a genuinely solid offline daily-loop, but the RN app is a thin read-only mirror that ignores every native affordance (camera/barcode/photo/health-kit) that defines best-in-class 2026 logging — leaving the single biggest adherence lever unbuilt.

topConcerns.title: expo-camera installed but ZERO capture affordance — the defining 2026 native gap
topConcerns.detail: Best-in-class logging in 2026 is multi-modal capture at the eating moment (barcode + photo + voice), correlated with ~3x higher logging rates vs manual. EVA has expo-camera in apps/mobile but no barcode scan, no photo log, no quick-add. The alumno can only tap-complete a coach-prescribed meal. This is the highest-impact missing native feature: a barcode scanner alone (FatSecret/Open Food Facts lookup) would move EVA from 'check the box' to real capture. Mobile is exactly where this must live — web can't scan a package in a kitchen.

topConcerns.title: RN app is a read-only mirror: no exchanges module, no coach swaps, no off-plan logging
topConcerns.detail: Mobile parity is broken in 3 load-bearing ways: (1) the entire exchanges module — the literal paid 'metodo de nutricionistas' pitch — is absent on mobile; (2) coach-bounded food swaps are fetched but dead-wired (activeSwapMealIds unused), so the alumno sees swaps they cannot apply; (3) no off-plan / free-food entry at all. The professional product is sold on exchanges and the consumer retention lever is honest capture; neither exists where 80%+ of clients actually log (their phone).

topConcerns.title: Mobile reads live targets, not the snapshotted target_*_at_log — historical adherence is wrong on phone
topConcerns.detail: The schema correctly snapshots target_protein_g_at_log etc. into daily_nutrition_logs and the web reads them, but mobile reads live plan targets for historical days. After any coach plan edit, a client scrolling back a week on their phone sees adherence computed against the NEW targets — a different, wrong number than the coach sees on web. The snapshot columns are written-but-unread on mobile. Same data, two answers, and the phone is the one the client trusts.

topConcerns.title: Adherence has 2 formulas / 4 definitions; mobile widgets/notifications would amplify the divergence
topConcerns.detail: client-detail.service counts done/total over ALL meal logs (no day-of-week filter) while dashboard.service filters by Santiago weekday — so ring, strip, KPI and today-badge legitimately disagree for one student. As soon as this is surfaced in a home-screen widget or a push body ('you're at 60% today'), the number must be ONE canonical value computed once server-side and passed down. Shipping native surfaces on top of the current split-brain will harden a confusing number into the client's lock screen.

topConcerns.title: Reminder cron is unbatched/un-rate-limited and fires with nothing prescribed — push at scale will time out and annoy
topConcerns.detail: nutrition-reminder is a single long invocation, no batching/rate-limit (Vercel timeout risk at 300-client team scale), and fires even when nothing is planned for today. Best-practice 2026 notifications trigger at the client's OWN habitual mealtime and ~30min after a missed window, never a global hour, and never when there's nothing to do. Current behavior trains users to disable EVA notifications — the opposite of the adherence lever it's meant to be.

topConcerns.title: No native health/wearable sync — Apple Health / Health Connect untouched
topConcerns.detail: 2026 bar includes auto-import of expenditure/weight/sleep from Apple Health, Garmin, Health Connect, plus CGM (Libre/Dexcom) for metabolic clients. EVA's RN app ingests none of it. Weight trend is the input adaptive-TDEE needs; without HealthKit/Health Connect the coach is stuck on manual check-in weigh-ins. This is platform-native plumbing (HealthKit entitlement + Health Connect permissions) that only the mobile app can provide.

topConcerns.title: No adherence-neutral framing on the native streak/confetti loop — gameable and potentially shame-inducing
topConcerns.detail: The mobile loop rewards marking-done with streak + confetti + haptics, where 'done' credits full planned macros regardless of what was eaten. 2026 best practice (MacroFactor) is explicitly adherence-NEUTRAL: restartable 'logged 5 of 7 days', no all-or-nothing chain, no red over-budget. The current all-or-nothing streak both reinforces dishonest self-report and risks the shame-spiral that makes users stop logging for the day. Needs reframing before it scales.

recommendations.title: Ship a barcode scanner on mobile wired to a region-layered food lookup
recommendations.detail: Use the already-installed expo-camera (or expo-barcode-scanner) to scan EAN/UPC, resolve against Open Food Facts (free, Chile portal) first then FatSecret Platform API (Latam brands/barcodes) with source+verification tags, and write to a new off-plan intake row. This is the single highest-leverage native feature and the foundation for honest logging. Keep it assistive: show the matched product for one-tap confirm before saving.

recommendations.title: Add off-plan / free-food logging on mobile (the data model gap behind everything)
recommendations.detail: Introduce an intake row decoupled from prescription so the alumno can log what they actually ate (barcode result, quick-add, recent/copy-meal) separate from coach-prescribed meals. This is the prerequisite for real intake-vs-target adherence and for barcode/photo capture to have somewhere to land. Label coach-prescription adherence and real intake as two distinct numbers in the UI.

recommendations.title: Make mobile read snapshotted targets and consume ONE server-computed adherence value
recommendations.detail: Read target_*_at_log for historical days on mobile (columns already populated) and have RSC/server compute a single canonical adherence number passed to every surface (web, mobile, widget, push) using the day-of-week-filtered Santiago definition. Kill the client-detail vs dashboard formula divergence before adding any native surface. Small change, removes a whole class of 'the app says X, my coach says Y' tickets.

recommendations.title: Wire the dead coach-swap path on mobile (activeSwapMealIds)
recommendations.detail: The swap data is already fetched; connect activeSwapMealIds so the alumno can apply coach-bounded swaps in NutritionShell with optimistic UI + the existing offline queue. Showing swaps the user can't tap is worse than not showing them. Reuse the shared macro-transform util so web/mobile/PDF stay consistent.

recommendations.title: Rebuild the reminder cron as batched, rate-limited, prescription-aware, and mealtime-contextual
recommendations.detail: Batch recipients, rate-limit via the existing Upstash limiter, skip clients with nothing prescribed today, and trigger at the client's own habitual mealtime (or ~30min after a missed window) rather than a global hour — fall back to ~18:00 Santiago. Add per-row error isolation so one bad subscription doesn't abort the tenant run, and basic alerting (not console-only) on cron failures.

recommendations.title: Reframe the native streak/confetti loop to adherence-neutral
recommendations.detail: Replace the all-or-nothing chain with restartable 'X of 7 days' consistency, drop red over-budget styling, and never fire a shame pop-up. Pair every macro ring color with a text label (WCAG 2.2 AA, color-blind safe) so the ring isn't the only signal. Cheap, high-retention, aligns with MacroFactor's proven model.

recommendations.title: Bring the exchanges (intercambios) module to mobile read-at-minimum
recommendations.detail: Port the pure exchange-calc/reconcile domain (already IO-free and shared-friendly) so the alumno can at least VIEW their intercambios plan and log portions on mobile. The paid nutritionist pitch is invisible on the device clients use most. Start read+log of meal_exchange_targets and day variants; defer authoring to web.

recommendations.title: Add Apple Health / Health Connect weight + bodyweight sync
recommendations.detail: Request HealthKit (iOS) and Health Connect (Android) read permissions for body mass and import into the check-in weight trend. This feeds the adaptive-TDEE story and removes manual weigh-in entry. Scope tightly to weight first; defer activity/CGM. Native-only capability that differentiates the app from the web PWA.

recommendations.title: Add AI photo logging as a confirm/correct draft (after off-plan logging exists)
recommendations.detail: Once off-plan intake lands, add expo-camera photo capture that produces a per-item draft with confidence indicators, reconciled against the verified food DB, one-tap correction. Treat as assistive only — Latam/Chilean dishes drop AI accuracy to 60-75%, so never auto-commit. This is the 2026 frontier beyond barcode; sequence it after the data model and barcode ship.

recommendations.title: Add a home-screen / lock-screen quick-log widget and recents/copy-meal
recommendations.detail: Put logging in front of the user with near-zero tax to start (the #1 photo-logging failure is forgetting before eating). iOS WidgetKit + Android Glance widget showing today's ring/bars and a one-tap relog of recent meals. Depends on the canonical adherence value and off-plan logging being in place first.

quickWins: Wire activeSwapMealIds in NutritionShell so already-fetched coach swaps become tappable on mobile (data is there, just dead-wired).
Make mobile read target_*_at_log for historical days instead of live plan targets — columns are already populated, fixes wrong historical adherence on phone.
Skip the nutrition-reminder push when nothing is prescribed for the client today — stops training users to disable EVA notifications.
Reframe the native streak to restartable 'X of 7 days' and drop red over-budget styling — small UI change, adherence-neutral per 2026 best practice.
Pair every macro ring color with a text label on mobile for WCAG 2.2 AA / color-blind safety.
Standardize macro colors between page and dashboard (orange/blue/yellow vs rose/amber/emerald) so the same nutrient isn't two colors across surfaces.

roleFindings.verdict: The nutrition system ships features but is operationally blind and unsafe at scale: console-only crons with no alerting, transaction-less bulk/propagation jobs that corrupt data on partial failure, no data-quality job in CI, and grant gaps that allow cross-tenant re-scoping — none of it would survive a 300-client team onboarding.

topConcerns.title: Crons are observability dark matter — console-only, no alerting, no heartbeat
topConcerns.detail: nutrition-reminder and nutrition-cycles log to console only. If either silently stops (Vercel cron misconfig, timeout, thrown error), nobody is paged: students stop getting reminders and periodization blocks stop advancing, discovered only by user complaint. For a 2-founder on-call there is no dead-man's-switch, no last-run record, no error metric. This is the single highest-leverage operational gap.

topConcerns.title: Bulk-assign and propagation are transaction-less and corrupt data on partial failure
topConcerns.detail: assignOrgNutritionPlanTemplateToClientsAction deactivates all prior plans then inserts new ones with no transaction; propagateTemplateChanges loops clients serially with awaited queries, no Promise.all, ignoring most errors. A mid-run failure on a 300-client team leaves some clients with zero active plan and others mutated — silent, unbounded blast radius, exactly the team-scale onboarding EVA is preparing for.

topConcerns.title: Production data corruption (empty meals/plans) has no preventive guard and no CI/cron detection
topConcerns.detail: The 2026-04-30 audit found 17 empty meals and 2 entirely-empty-but-active plans still accruing daily logs, feeding adherence math and PDFs. The detection script (audit-nutrition-empty-meals.mjs) is manual — not in CI, not a cron, no write-time guard. Corruption can recur and go unnoticed; the known bad rows are still live.

topConcerns.title: Scope columns on nutrition_plans and foods are UPDATE-grantable to authenticated
topConcerns.detail: Contrary to the clients/teams hardening doctrine in CLAUDE.md, client_id/coach_id/org_id on nutrition_plans and foods are not grant-revoked — cross-tenant re-scoping is gated only by RLS WITH CHECK. foods has no column allowlist, so a coach could flip coach_id→NULL to leak a private food toward the global pool. Not covered by the existing module-grants drift suite.

topConcerns.title: No per-row error isolation in cycle automation; reminder cron unbatched/un-rate-limited
topConcerns.detail: One bad row aborts the whole tenant's cycle-advance run; the reminder cron is a single unbatched invocation that fires even when nothing is prescribed today, risking a Vercel timeout and wasted push/Upstash quota at team scale.

topConcerns.title: History is non-reproducible — food edits retroactively rewrite past logs
topConcerns.detail: Computed intake reads live from foods at calc time with only plan-level snapshots; editing a food silently changes every past log's numbers, so adherence analytics can't be audited or reproduced. Grams-mode reconciliation also keys on positional order_index, so a reorder+save can reattach a student's historical logs to a different meal.

recommendations.title: Add structured logging + alerting + heartbeats to both nutrition crons
recommendations.detail: Wrap each cron in a run wrapper that emits a structured start/finish record (rows processed, errors, duration) and pings a dead-man's-switch (Better Stack / Healthchecks.io) on success. Route any thrown error or non-zero error count to Sentry plus a Slack/email alert. Persist last-run + outcome to a cron_runs table so /admin can show 'nutrition-cycles: 3h ago, 412 advanced, 0 errors'. Turns silent failure into a paged incident.

recommendations.title: Move bulk-assign and propagation into transactional, idempotent, set-based RPCs
recommendations.detail: Reimplement deactivate-then-insert and the propagation loop as SECURITY DEFINER service-role Postgres functions running in one transaction so partial failure rolls back. Batch set-based instead of per-client awaits; add an idempotency key per run and a reconciliation query flagging any client without exactly one active plan. Prerequisite for any team-scale assign.

recommendations.title: Promote the empty-meal audit to CI check + nightly cron + write-time guard
recommendations.detail: Add a CHECK/trigger blocking activation of a plan with zero non-empty meals and blocking save of an empty meal. Run the audit script nightly with alerting on any hit, and add a fast SQL/vitest assertion in CI against seed data. Backfill-fix the known 17 corrupt rows before the next onboarding.

recommendations.title: Lock down scope-column grants on nutrition_plans and foods and extend the drift suite
recommendations.detail: REVOKE UPDATE ON nutrition_plans/foods FROM authenticated + GRANT UPDATE on an explicit non-scope allowlist (mirroring clients/teams), making client_id/coach_id/org_id service-role-only. Add both tables to tests/separation/module-grants.sql so drift is caught in CI. Migration additive/idempotent per branching doctrine.

recommendations.title: Batch + rate-limit + early-exit the reminder cron
recommendations.detail: Chunk students with a concurrency cap aligned to Upstash limits, short-circuit students with nothing prescribed today (via nutritionMealAppliesOnIsoYmdInSantiago before enqueue), and consider a scheduled fan-out so no single invocation nears the Vercel timeout. Emit per-batch metrics to cron_runs.

recommendations.title: Add per-row try/catch + quarantine to cycle automation
recommendations.detail: Isolate each cycle-row advancement so one bad row is logged, counted, and skipped rather than aborting the tenant run; surface quarantined rows in the cron alert for manual follow-up.

recommendations.title: Snapshot food macros per log and key grams reconciliation by id
recommendations.detail: Persist food macros into the log row at log time (or version foods) so historical intake is reproducible and auditable, and switch grams-mode reconciliation from order_index to meal id (parity with the exchanges fix). Both changes forward-only/additive.

recommendations.title: Build a single /admin nutrition-ops health panel
recommendations.detail: Once cron_runs exists, surface last-run status, error counts, empty-plan count, and grant-drift status for nutrition in one admin panel so the founder on-call has a single pane of glass instead of grepping Vercel logs.

quickWins: Wire a free dead-man's-switch (Healthchecks.io / Better Stack) ping into both nutrition crons — minutes of work, instantly detects a cron that stopped running.
Add early-exit in nutrition-reminder for students with nothing prescribed today — cuts wasted push quota and notification fatigue immediately.
Backfill-fix the 17 known empty meals + 2 empty active plans from the 2026-04-30 audit before the next onboarding.
Add nutrition_plans and foods to tests/separation/module-grants.sql so the existing CI drift check starts covering them.
Replace console.log in both crons with one structured log line (rows/errors/duration) routed to Sentry on error — a single wrapper function.

roleFindings.verdict: Pure-calc layer is genuinely well-tested; everything around it (the grams build→assign→log→review flow, adherence-formula agreement, RLS on legacy nutrition tables, data-integrity invariants, mobile parity) is under-tested or untested — coverage is inverted relative to risk, and nothing runs in CI to catch regressions.

topConcerns.title: Coverage is inverted: deterministic math is tested, the money/trust paths are smoke-only
topConcerns.detail: 17 nutrition unit specs cover the IO-free core well (exchange-calc, nutrition-utils with the liquid serving_size case, reconcile with the critical orphan-log/reorder cases, cycle resolver, offline queue, PDF brand). But the entire legacy grams flow — build a plan, assign, alumno logs a meal, coach reviews adherence — has NO end-to-end test. tests/nutrition-student-smoke.spec.ts is 2 assertions (heading renders, offline banner). The exchanges path is the only flow with real E2E (separation/nutrition-exchanges.spec.ts, ~12 tests A/B/C/D incl PDF brand, offline queue, day variants). So the newer, narrower, paid module is well-covered while the default path most coaches use is not. Regression risk on the grams path is effectively unguarded.

topConcerns.title: No test pins the adherence contract — 2 formulas, 4 definitions, zero agreement assertions
topConcerns.detail: client-detail.service counts done/total over ALL meal logs (no day-of-week filter); dashboard.service + the today% filter via nutritionMealAppliesOnIsoYmdInSantiago. These can legitimately disagree for the same student on the same screen, and there is no test asserting any relationship between them. nutritionMealAppliesOnIsoYmdInSantiago (the Santiago-timezone day-of-week gate that drives which meals count today) has no dedicated DST/timezone unit test despite being the highest-leverage correctness function in the feature. A 'consumed = planned × completion%' fallback (calculateConsumedMacrosWithCompletionFallback) is also untested against the off-plan reality it silently models. This is the bug class most likely to reach production undetected because every path is green.

topConcerns.title: RLS isolation tested for exchanges/team tables but NOT for legacy grams + logging tables
topConcerns.detail: team/exchanges-isolation.sql and team-isolation.sql + separation/*.sql cover exchange_groups, meal_exchange_targets, day_variants, template team_id, and pdf-log. There is no dedicated tx-rollback isolation suite asserting a coach cannot read/write another tenant's nutrition_plans, nutrition_meals, food_items, daily_nutrition_logs, nutrition_meal_logs, or that scope columns (client_id/coach_id/org_id) can't be re-pointed cross-tenant via PATCH. Given the map flags these columns as UPDATE-grantable to authenticated and foods having no column allowlist (coach_id→NULL promotion risk), this is an untested cross-tenant write surface. Also: every SQL suite is gate-only and manual (working-rule 2026-06-10) — none runs in CI, so RLS regressions from any future migration ship silently until the next authorized gate.

topConcerns.title: Known data-corruption modes have no regression test and no CI guard
topConcerns.detail: The 2026-04-30 empty-meal audit (audit-nutrition-empty-meals.mjs) found 17 empty meals + 2 fully-empty plans still accruing daily logs. That script is manual, not in CI/cron, and there is no unit/integration test asserting 'empty meal cannot be saved' or 'empty plan cannot be activated', nor a DB invariant. Likewise no test covers: destructive org bulk-assign (assignOrgNutritionPlanTemplateToClientsAction deactivates ALL active org clients, no transaction, partial-failure leaves zero active plan), serial transaction-less propagateTemplateChanges (300-client failure mid-loop), or food-edit retroactively rewriting historical log macros. These are exactly the silent-data-loss bugs a coach reports weeks later as 'my numbers changed'.

topConcerns.title: Mobile nutrition has near-zero automated coverage despite being the alumno's primary surface
topConcerns.detail: apps/mobile nutrition (offline AsyncStorage read-model, toggle queue drain on reconnect+foreground, optimistic logging) is validated only by manual visual audits (enterprise/mobile-visual-audit.spec.ts is a screenshot pass, not behavioral). Documented parity gaps — alumno can't apply coach swaps (activeSwapMealIds dead-wired), exchanges module absent, historical days render live targets instead of the written target_*_at_log snapshot — are precisely the kind of regressions a Detox/Maestro flow would catch and that no current test does. The single biggest unused native affordance (expo-camera installed, no barcode/photo) also means no harness exists for the capability the best-practice bar now treats as table stakes.

topConcerns.title: Two food-create and two food-browse paths with duplicated validation/rounding, untested for parity
topConcerns.detail: FoodSearchDrawer.addCoachCustomFood (JSON) vs AddFoodSheet.saveCustomFood (FormData) duplicate validation + INTEGER rounding; FoodSearchDrawer vs FoodBrowser duplicate browse. No test asserts the two create paths produce identical persisted rows, so they can drift (one rounds, one floors; one trims a field, one doesn't) without any failing test. Same risk for the two live serialization formats (legacy FormData actions vs JSON actions) and the split-brain persistence (atomic Save for grams vs 700ms debounce auto-save for exchanges vs separate cycle action) — there is no 'is this plan saved' invariant test.

recommendations.title: Build one golden E2E for the grams flow: build → assign → alumno logs → coach review
recommendations.detail: Add a Playwright spec mirroring the structure of separation/nutrition-exchanges.spec.ts but for the default grams path: coach creates a plan with meals/foods, assigns to a seeded alumno, alumno marks a meal complete at a known portion%, then assert the SAME adherence number appears consistently on the Overview ring, the Nutrición strip, the dashboard KPI, and the today badge. This single test simultaneously closes the grams-E2E gap AND becomes the executable contract for the adherence formula. Seed via the existing seed-e2e-personas.mjs personas.

recommendations.title: Pin the adherence/day-applicability contract with fast unit tests before any UI refactor
recommendations.detail: Add a unit suite for nutritionMealAppliesOnIsoYmdInSantiago covering DST transitions, week boundaries, day-of-week NULL (every-day meals), and Santiago vs UTC edge dates. Add a property-style test asserting the documented relationship between client-detail.service (all-logs) and dashboard.service (day-filtered) adherence — even if the assertion is 'they may differ ONLY by day-of-week filtering', encode it so a future change that makes them diverge for another reason fails. Add tests for calculateConsumedMacrosWithCompletionFallback at 0/25/50/75/100% and the empty/null plan case.

recommendations.title: Write a grams + logging RLS isolation SQL suite and add data-integrity invariant SQL to the gate
recommendations.detail: Create tests/separation/nutrition-grams-isolation.sql (tx + ROLLBACK, impersonate authenticated with claims, never service_role) asserting cross-tenant SELECT/UPDATE denial on nutrition_plans, nutrition_meals, food_items, daily_nutrition_logs, nutrition_meal_logs, and explicitly that a coach cannot UPDATE client_id/coach_id/org_id to another tenant nor flip foods.coach_id→NULL. In the same file add invariant asserts: no active plan with zero meals, no meal with zero food_items feeding a daily log. Model it on team/exchanges-isolation.sql which already has the seed/rollback pattern.

recommendations.title: Promote the empty-meal audit and the RLS/invariant SQL suites into CI (advisory) + a weekly cron
recommendations.detail: The working-rule keeps heavy Playwright/SQL gate-only, but the empty-meal corruption and RLS drift are exactly what silent CI guards exist for. Convert audit-nutrition-empty-meals.mjs into a read-only check that runs nightly against prod (or on a Supabase branch) and alerts on count>0, and run the *.sql isolation suites on a Supabase preview branch in a non-blocking CI job so migration-induced RLS regressions surface at PR time instead of the next manual gate. Wire cron observability (the map flags console-only, no alerting) so a failed nutrition-cycles/reminder run pages someone.

recommendations.title: Add regression tests for the destructive write paths before they touch a 300-client team
recommendations.detail: Integration tests (against a Supabase branch, in the gate) for: (a) org bulk-assign rolls back fully on partial failure and never leaves a client with zero active plan, and only targets the intended subset the UI implies; (b) propagateTemplateChanges is transactional/idempotent and a mid-loop error leaves no half-mutated client set; (c) editing a food does NOT retroactively change a historical nutrition_meal_log's computed macros (this test will FAIL today and is the lever to force per-log macro snapshotting). Failing-first tests here are the cheapest insurance against the Movida-scale incident.

recommendations.title: Stand up a mobile behavioral harness (Maestro or Detox) for the alumno nutrition loop
recommendations.detail: One Maestro flow: open nutrition offline, toggle a meal, kill+reopen app, reconnect, assert the toggle drained exactly once (the dedup queue). Add a parity assertion that mobile reads target_*_at_log on historical days (currently it doesn't — test fails first, documents the bug). When barcode/photo lands, this harness is where the capture-at-eating-moment flow gets covered. Maestro is lower-setup than Detox for Expo and runs the same flow on the screenshot CI you already have.

recommendations.title: Add parity/golden tests for duplicated food + serialization paths to stop silent drift
recommendations.detail: A table-driven unit test feeding the same input through addCoachCustomFood (JSON) and saveCustomFood (FormData) and asserting byte-identical persisted shape (same rounding, same trimmed fields). Same for the two browse components' filter/sort, and a serialization round-trip test for legacy FormData vs JSON plan actions. These are cheap, fast, and convert 'two code paths that happen to agree today' into 'two code paths a test forces to agree.'

recommendations.title: Gate the 4-macro→micronutrient and intake-vs-adherence expansion with contract tests written first
recommendations.detail: Before the clinical data-model work the other lenses will demand (fiber/sodium/sugar/sat-fat/micros, real off-plan intake), write the calc contract tests now: kcal cross-check (4P+4C+9F within tolerance of stated kcal), protein-floor and kcal upper/lower safety bounds, and an explicit 'consumed = plan-adherence, NOT intake' labeled assertion so the moment real-intake logging lands the test forces the UI to stop implying tracked macros. Writing these as failing/skipped specs now makes the eventual feature spec executable and prevents the safety rails from being an afterthought.

quickWins: Add a unit test for nutritionMealAppliesOnIsoYmdInSantiago covering DST + day-of-week NULL — highest-leverage correctness function, currently untested.
Extend nutrition-student-smoke.spec.ts to actually toggle a meal and assert the today% updates — turns a render-smoke into a behavior check for ~10 lines.
Add an assertion to the existing reconcile test that grams-mode reconciliation matches by id, not order_index (will fail today, documenting the positional-key bug already fixed in exchanges).
Run audit-nutrition-empty-meals.mjs now and triage the 2 empty-plan clients from the 2026-04-30 finding before they keep accruing bad adherence data.
Add a one-line invariant query to the next authorized gate: assert zero active nutrition_plans with zero meals and zero meals feeding a daily log.
Snapshot-test calculateConsumedMacrosWithCompletionFallback at 0/25/50/75/100% and null-plan so the 'consumed' fallback can't silently change.

roleFindings.verdict: Functional but not isolation-safe: column-level UPDATE grants on nutrition_plans/foods let a coach re-scope rows across tenants, and the product handles unlabeled health PII with no clinical-data hardening — defensible for macros, not yet for clinical-grade nutrition.

topConcerns.title: Tenant scope columns are UPDATE-grantable to authenticated on nutrition_plans and foods
topConcerns.detail: Per the current-state map (weakness #7) and contrary to the clients-hardening doctrine in CLAUDE.md ('scoping is service-role-only'), nutrition_plans (client_id/coach_id/org_id) and foods (coach_id, no column allowlist at all) expose scope columns to authenticated UPDATE, gated only by RLS WITH CHECK rather than grant revocation. RLS WITH CHECK is a weaker control than absent grant: any policy gap, future policy regression, or permissive predicate becomes a cross-tenant re-scoping primitive. Concretely a coach can flip foods.coach_id -> NULL to push a private food toward the global 316-row pool (data poisoning of a shared catalog read by every tenant), or attempt to move a nutrition_plan's client_id/org_id. This is the canonical PostgREST 42501 trap inverted — the grant exists, so only the policy stands between the actor and the write. Health prescriptions and a shared food catalog are exactly the rows that must be service-role-only to write-scope.

topConcerns.title: Health-data PII (nutrition prescriptions, logs, habits) has no clinical-grade handling or provenance
topConcerns.detail: nutrition_plans/daily_nutrition_logs/nutrition_meal_logs/daily_habits/client_food_preferences are health data under Chile's Ley 21.719 (datos sensibles de salud, in force 2026). They are stored, snapshotted into nutrition_plan_history jsonb, and exported to PDF with no field-level classification, no access audit trail on read, and no allergy/intolerance enforcement (client_food_preferences only pins favorites — an allergen is never blocked at prescribe time). Food edits retroactively rewrite history (weakness #9), so a coach cannot reproduce what was prescribed/consumed — fatal for any clinical dispute or SERNAC-style evidence requirement (the billing layer already has billing_snapshots for exactly this reason; nutrition has nothing equivalent at the log level). No verified/source provenance flag on foods means coach-typed macros are indistinguishable from an authoritative table — there is no integrity signal on health-affecting data.

topConcerns.title: Two parallel nutrition gates with no unifying resolver — entitlement bypass is one forgotten check away
topConcerns.detail: Weakness #10: tier canUseNutrition (inline in RSC) and enabled_modules/coach_addons via assertModule are independent gates, with the cross-dependency hand-enforced in only 2-3 call sites. The grams write path (NutritionService) bypasses nutrition.repository.ts and talks to Supabase directly (pillar violation), so any new write surface added there can reach the DB without passing the same module assertion the exchanges path centralizes via assertExchangesModuleForPlan/assertModule. This is an authorization-completeness gap: defense lives in application code at the edge, not at a single choke point, and the data layer does not re-assert entitlement.

topConcerns.title: Org bulk-assign and propagation are transaction-less and destructive — integrity/availability risk
topConcerns.detail: Weaknesses #5 and #6: assignOrgNutritionPlanTemplateToClientsAction unconditionally deactivates all prior plans for ALL active org clients (UI implies a subset) and inserts macro-only plans with no transaction; propagateTemplateChanges loops clients serially, swallows most insert/update errors, no Promise.all, no DB transaction. A mid-loop failure leaves clients with zero active plan (a denial-of-prescription state that still accrues daily logs). From a security/integrity lens this is an unbounded, non-atomic, partial-failure-prone mutation over health records triggered by a single coach action — both a data-integrity and an availability concern at 300-client team scale.

topConcerns.title: Empty-plan / empty-meal corruption is unmonitored and feeds adherence + PDFs as valid
topConcerns.detail: Weakness #4: the 2026-04-30 audit found entirely empty plans still accruing daily logs; the check (audit-nutrition-empty-meals.mjs) is manual, not in CI/cron, and nothing prevents saving an empty meal or activating an empty plan. This is unvalidated state on health records that silently propagates into adherence math and branded PDF deliverables. No invariant enforcement at write time, no observability — a data-quality control gap that becomes a credibility/liability issue once output carries the coach's brand and a legal disclaimer.

topConcerns.title: Cron automation has no observability, no per-row isolation, and unbatched fan-out
topConcerns.detail: Weakness #12: nutrition-reminder and nutrition-cycles are console-only with no alerting; one bad row aborts the whole tenant run (no per-row error isolation); the reminder cron is unbatched/un-rate-limited (single long invocation risks Vercel timeout at scale) and fires even with nothing prescribed. Security-relevant because these jobs run with elevated (service-role) reach across all tenants on a schedule, with no audit of what they touched and no failure signal — a silent failure or partial run over multi-tenant health data is undetectable today.

topConcerns.title: Positional meal reconciliation in grams mode can rebind a client's logs to the wrong meal
topConcerns.detail: Weakness #8: grams-mode reconciliation keys on order_index (positional); reorder+save can attach a client's historical adherence logs to a different meal. The exchanges path was explicitly fixed to match by id; grams was not. This is integrity corruption of health-log attribution caused by a routine coach edit — the kind of silent cross-record contamination that undermines the auditability of consumed-vs-prescribed data.

recommendations.title: Revoke table-level UPDATE on nutrition_plans/foods and re-grant a column allowlist excluding all scope columns
recommendations.detail: Mirror the clients/teams doctrine exactly: REVOKE UPDATE ON nutrition_plans FROM authenticated; GRANT UPDATE (daily_calories, protein_g, carbs_g, fats_g, plan_mode, is_active, ...editable cols) TO authenticated — omitting client_id/coach_id/org_id/template_id so re-scoping is service-role-only. Do the same on foods, explicitly omitting coach_id (close the coach_id->NULL global-pool poisoning path). Add these to the tests/separation/module-grants.sql drift suite so column_privileges is asserted in CI. Migration must be aditiva/forward-only per the branching protocol. This converts a policy-dependent control into a grant-absent control.

recommendations.title: Add a per-log nutrient snapshot and a foods provenance/verification flag
recommendations.detail: Snapshot the consumed macro/nutrient values into nutrition_meal_logs (or a sibling) at log time — the same pattern target_*_at_log already uses for targets — so food edits stop retroactively rewriting history and adherence becomes reproducible/auditable (the nutrition analogue of billing_snapshots).
