Add foods.source (e.g. inta|fatsecret|usda|coach_custom) + verified boolean, default coach_custom/false; rank verified/local first in search and stamp provenance on PDFs. This gives health-affecting data an integrity signal and makes any clinical dispute defensible.

recommendations.title: Unify the nutrition entitlement gate behind one server-side resolver enforced at the data boundary
recommendations.detail: Collapse tier canUseNutrition and enabled_modules/coach_addons into a single assertNutritionAccess(actor, planMode) called once per write path, and route the grams write path through nutrition.repository.ts so the data layer (not just RSC edges) re-asserts entitlement — eliminating the bypass NutritionService creates today. One choke point closes the 'check one gate, forget the other' authorization-completeness gap.

recommendations.title: Make org bulk-assign and propagation atomic, selective, and per-row isolated
recommendations.detail: Move the destructive deactivate+insert into a single Postgres RPC (SECURITY DEFINER, service-role) wrapped in a transaction so partial failure rolls back and no client is left plan-less; make the target set the explicit subset the UI shows, not all-active. Convert propagateTemplateChanges to a set-based/transactional RPC or batched Promise.all with per-row error capture and a returned failure report, never silent swallowing.

recommendations.title: Enforce non-empty-plan invariants at write time + add a daily integrity cron with alerting
recommendations.detail: Add a CHECK/trigger (or service-layer guard) blocking activation of a plan with zero meals and saving a meal with zero food_items/exchange targets. Promote audit-nutrition-empty-meals.mjs into a scheduled job that emits a real alert (not console) on drift, and extend it to flag plans accruing logs while empty. Invariant + observability turns a silent corruption into a caught-at-write error.

recommendations.title: Harden crons: structured logging + alerting, per-row try/catch isolation, batched/rate-limited reminder fan-out
recommendations.detail: Wrap each tenant/row in the cycle and reminder jobs in isolated error handling so one bad row never aborts the run; emit structured success/failure counts to an alertable sink; batch the reminder cron and skip clients with nothing prescribed today to avoid Vercel timeouts and useless pushes. These jobs run with service-role reach across all tenants — they must be observable and fault-isolated.

recommendations.title: Switch grams-mode meal reconciliation to id-based matching (parity with exchanges)
recommendations.detail: Replace order_index positional reconciliation with stable meal-id matching, exactly as the exchanges path was fixed, so reorder+save can never rebind a client's historical logs to a different meal. Backfill/verify existing logs are correctly attributed.

recommendations.title: Classify nutrition tables as datos sensibles de salud and add read-access auditing on cross-actor reads
recommendations.detail: Tag nutrition_plans/daily_nutrition_logs/nutrition_meal_logs/daily_habits/client_food_preferences as health PII (Ley 21.719). Log access when an actor other than the owning client reads them (coach/org-admin viewing a student), retain in an append-only audit (reuse the org_audit_logs/audit_log_checksums pattern), and add allergen/intolerance enforcement so client_food_preferences can block a prescribed allergen rather than merely pin favorites — turning a passive preference into an active safety control.

quickWins: REVOKE table-level UPDATE + GRANT a scope-excluding column allowlist on nutrition_plans and foods (one aditiva migration); add to module-grants.sql drift suite.
Add foods.source + foods.verified columns (default coach_custom/false) to stamp provenance on health-affecting macro data.
Switch grams-mode meal reconciliation from order_index to id-based matching to stop log misattribution on reorder.
Add a CHECK/trigger blocking activation of zero-meal plans and saving zero-item meals.
Promote audit-nutrition-empty-meals.mjs from manual script to a scheduled job with a real alert on drift.
Wrap each row of the cycle/reminder crons in per-row try/catch so one bad row no longer aborts the tenant run.

roleFindings.verdict: EVA today is a competent macro-and-exchange tool for personal-training coaches, but it sells "the nutritionist method" while shipping the artifact (a branded PDF) without the reasoning (assessment to requirement to adequacy to monitoring) a real RD needs — and fragmentation, the 4-macro ceiling, and intake-vs-adherence conflation cap it below clinical-grade and below what wins deals.

topConcerns.title: We sell a nutritionist product we don't have (positioning vs reality gap)
topConcerns.detail: The paid 'el metodo de los nutricionistas' pitch sells a tidy branded exchange PDF (the artifact), not the assessment-to-requirement-to-adequacy-to-monitoring reasoning a licensed RD needs. A real nutritionist cannot manage a single clinical case (no fiber/sodium/sugar/micros), cannot trust the food data (coach-typed, INTEGER-rounded, no verified source), and cannot drive targets off assessment. This is a credibility and arguably a SERNAC/consumer-claim exposure, not just a feature gap. It is the P0 because it shapes pricing, messaging, and what we can defensibly charge for.

topConcerns.title: Adherence is shown 4 incompatible ways simultaneously and the numbers don't reconcile
topConcerns.detail: Two divergent formulas (client-detail counts all logs with no day-of-week filter; dashboard filters by applicable day) plus four definitions (logged-1-meal heatmap / % of plan meals / weekly-avg ring / today %) render on the same student and legitimately disagree. The UI ships apologetic tooltips to excuse it. This is the fastest way to lose a coach's trust in the entire data layer, and every new nutrition surface multiplies the inconsistency. Must be fixed before building anything new on top.

topConcerns.title: Consumed = plan-adherence, not real intake — the core coach job-to-be-done is unmet and the metric is gameable
topConcerns.detail: Completion credits full planned macros; rings can read 100% while the client ate something entirely different. There is no off-plan entry, no >100% portion, no barcode, and expo-camera is installed but wired to nothing. Streak/heatmap reward marking-done, which can reinforce dishonest self-report. The single most valuable thing a coach wants (what did my client actually eat) is the one thing EVA cannot answer, and the UI implies it can.

topConcerns.title: Nutrition has no canonical home — fragmented across 9 review entry points, 3 template stores, 2 food-create paths, 2 builders
topConcerns.detail: Coach weekly review forces hopping across an Overview ring + a training-card red dot + an often-suppressed top alert + a 1411-line Nutricion mega-tab + hidden Progreso charts + the separate builder. Three template tables (one dead-end, one rich-but-unused, one with no UI), two food-create paths, two food-browse UIs, and split-brain save state (atomic grams vs debounced exchanges vs cycles). This is a product-coherence problem that taxes every job (build, assign, review) and inflates every future build.

topConcerns.title: Assignment and propagation are destructive, non-selective, and transaction-less at exactly the scale teams need
topConcerns.detail: Org bulk-assign always targets ALL active clients (UI implies a subset), unconditionally deactivates prior plans, inserts macro-only mealless plans, and has no transaction (partial failure = clients with zero active plan). Propagation loops clients serially with no Promise.all/transaction and swallows errors. For a 300-client team this is thousands of serial round-trips and silent data corruption. Plus an unaddressed 2026-04-30 audit found empty-meal corruption already live. This blocks safely selling teams.

topConcerns.title: The Chile/Latam wedge is open but unbuilt: verified local food DB + native intercambios
topConcerns.detail: Every PT platform leaves the dietitian bar unfilled (verified DB, allergy filters, intercambios, branded meal-plan output). EVA has white-label PDF and a clean exchanges engine but no verified/source-tagged food data (no INTA/SAFOODS/FatSecret/barcode in the builder), exchanges are client-plan-only (no templates), and allergies aren't enforced (favorites only). This is the strategic differentiator that would serve coaches AND nutritionists in one Chile-native app — currently the biggest unrealized deal-winner.

topConcerns.title: Quiet correctness/security debt that rewrites history and allows cross-tenant re-scoping
topConcerns.detail: nutrition_plans and foods leave scope columns (client_id/coach_id/org_id) UPDATE-grantable to authenticated, contrary to the clients-hardening doctrine — a coach could flip foods.coach_id to NULL to push a private food toward the global pool. Grams-mode reconciliation keys on positional order_index (exchanges was fixed; grams wasn't), so reorder+save can mis-attach a client's historical logs. Food edits read live and retroactively rewrite all past logs (non-reproducible analytics). Low-visibility but real money/trust/security risk.

recommendations.title: Reframe positioning + scope a clinical track behind the nutrition add-on
recommendations.detail: Stop marketing 'el metodo de los nutricionistas' against the current feature set. Either (a) reposition the current paid module honestly as 'planes de intercambio con marca propia' for coaches, or (b) commit a Clinical track that extends foods with fiber+sodium+sugar+saturated/unsat fat first, then a micronutrient panel with DRI-vs-training-goal separation. PM call: ship (a) immediately as a messaging fix, fund (b) as the wedge. De-risks consumer-claim exposure and aligns price to value.

recommendations.title: Pick ONE adherence definition as the single source of truth
recommendations.detail: Define adherence once (recommend: % of plan meals applicable this period, day-of-week filtered, in one shared function consumed by web + mobile + dashboard + PDF). Delete the divergent client-detail formula and the apologetic tooltips. Small, high-trust win that makes every nutrition surface agree, and must land before any new nutrition feature.

recommendations.title: Label adherence vs intake honestly, then build real intake capture
recommendations.detail: Phase 1 (S): relabel 'consumido' as 'adherencia al plan' everywhere and make streaks restartable/consistency-framed (adherence-neutral UX, top retention lever). Phase 2 (XL): wire expo-camera to barcode + photo logging reconciled against a verified DB, allow off-plan/free-food entry and >100% portions. Converts EVA from a check-off tool into a real tracker and unlocks the coach's core job-to-be-done.

recommendations.title: Build the Chile/Latam food catalog as a layered, source-tagged DB
recommendations.detail: Add source + verified columns to foods. Ingest INTA/SAFOODS (Chilean foods, manual, no API) ranked first, layer FatSecret Platform API (Latam brands/barcodes), Open Food Facts (free barcode first-pass), USDA fallback; rank verified/local first. Wire Edamam/recipes INTO the builder and mobile food search instead of the orphan /coach/recipes surface. The credibility floor every serious nutritionist checks first and the strongest Chile-native moat vs US apps.

recommendations.title: Consolidate nutrition into one canonical coach home + one builder save model
recommendations.detail: Collapse the 9 review entry points into a single Nutricion tab that is the canonical home (one adherence number, plan + analytics, deep-edit links out only). Unify the three template stores to one scoped table; kill the dead org_nutrition_templates path or merge its meal_names. One dirty/saved state across grams+exchanges+cycles. One food-create path, one food-browse UI. Mostly deletion; collapses both build and review jobs into one place.

recommendations.title: Make assignment and propagation safe: selective, transactional, batched
recommendations.detail: Org bulk-assign must respect the selected subset, run in a transaction, and copy meals (not macro-only). propagateTemplateChanges must batch (Promise.all/RPC), be transactional, and surface per-row errors. Add a DB constraint/save-guard against empty meals and empty active plans, and promote the empty-meal audit into a cron with alerting. Correctness fixes that are table stakes before pushing teams.

recommendations.title: Ship native intercambios (7-grupo) + macro-matched allergy-aware swaps as the wedge
recommendations.detail: Adopt the Chilean Manual de Porciones de Intercambio (7 grupos, UDD 2021), distinct from US ADA lists. Add deterministic macro auto-scaling to per-client targets, one-tap equivalence swaps that respect allergies/dislikes (enforce client_food_preferences as a filter, not just favorites), and exchange-based TEMPLATES (today client-plan-only). Extend white-label branding to the output, not just the shell. The open wedge no PT platform fills: coaches AND nutritionists in one app.

recommendations.title: Close the column-grant and history-rewrite correctness gaps
recommendations.detail: Apply the clients-hardening doctrine to nutrition_plans and foods: REVOKE table UPDATE, GRANT only the editable column allowlist, make client_id/coach_id/org_id service-role-only (a coach can currently flip foods.coach_id to NULL toward the global pool). Key grams-mode reconciliation by id not order_index. Snapshot per-log macros so editing a food does not retroactively rewrite history. Quiet but real money/trust/security debt.

recommendations.title: Adaptive targets + check-in glue + contextual reminders for retention
recommendations.detail: Reverse-calculate TDEE from logged intake + weight trend (converges 2-3 weeks) instead of freezing the signup Mifflin value; gate the equation (Mifflin default, Cunningham/Katch-McArdle when body_composition LBM exists), finally connecting the siloed body_composition add-on to nutrition. Make check-in the glue across nutrition+training+photos. Fire reminders at the client's own mealtimes + ~30min after a missed window, batched/rate-limited, never when nothing is prescribed today.

quickWins: Relabel 'consumido' as 'adherencia al plan' across web/mobile/PDF — removes the misleading-tracking claim with a copy change
Collapse adherence to one shared, day-of-week-filtered formula and delete the divergent client-detail.service version + the apologetic InfoTooltips
Make streaks restartable/consistency-framed ('5 de 7 dias') instead of all-or-nothing — adherence-neutral UX, a top retention lever, near-zero code
Add a save-time guard rejecting empty meals and empty active plans; promote audit-nutrition-empty-meals.mjs into a cron with alerting
Fix org bulk-assign to respect the selected subset instead of always targeting ALL active org clients (UI already implies a subset)
Stop firing the nutrition reminder cron when nothing is prescribed for the client today
Wire the existing /coach/recipes Edamam search into the builder food-search drawer instead of leaving it an orphan surface
Add foods.source + verified columns and rank verified/local first — schema groundwork for the credibility floor
Surface the often-suppressed nutrition<60% top alert as its own line instead of letting the workout/checkin priority cascade silently hide it

roleFindings.verdict: EVA's daily logging loop is genuinely strong, but the coach-side nutrition experience is a fragmented, untrustworthy maze — 9 entry points, 4 conflicting "adherence" numbers, and a builder split across two food UIs and two persistence models — that reads as a personal-trainer macro tool wearing a nutritionist costume.

topConcerns.title: Nutrition has no canonical home — 9 fragmented entry points on the coach side
topConcerns.detail: Reviewing one student forces hopping across Dashboard KPI hint + hidden ClientStatsSheet tab + overloaded ProfileTabNav badge + Overview ring + a red 'Nutri. en riesgo' dot buried inside the TRAINING card + a top alert that gets silently suppressed by workout/checkin rules + 3 charts hidden under a body-composition pill + a 1411-line NutritionTabB5 mega-component mixing edit and analytics + outbound links to the builder. There is no single 'nutrition' surface a coach can point to. This is the dominant UX failure: the information architecture actively hides the feature the product is trying to sell as premium.

topConcerns.title: Four incompatible 'adherence' numbers shown simultaneously, with apologetic tooltips
topConcerns.detail: On one set of screens 'adherence' means: logged >=1 meal (heatmap) / % of plan meals today (strip) / weekly avg % (ring) / all-meal-logs done-over-total with NO day-of-week filter (client-detail). Two divergent formulas across services mean the Overview ring, Nutricion strip, dashboard KPI and today badge can legitimately disagree for the same student on the same day. Shipping InfoTooltips to apologize for the discrepancy is a UX smell: the design is papering over a data-model problem. Conflicting numbers destroy coach trust faster than any missing feature.

topConcerns.title: The logging loop rewards marking-done, not eating — and the UI implies real tracking
topConcerns.detail: 'Consumed' macros = planned macros x completion%. Rings can read 100% while the client ate something else entirely; streak/confetti/heatmap reward checking boxes, which is gameable and can reinforce dishonest self-report. The visualization (rings + bars in real macro colors) reads as intake tracking but is actually plan-adherence. This is a labeling/honesty problem in the UI: best-practice 2026 apps frame adherence neutrally AND separate 'logged vs ate'. EVA conflates them while looking like MyFitnessPal.

topConcerns.title: Macro visualization is inconsistent and not accessibility-safe
topConcerns.detail: Macro colors differ between the page (orange/blue/yellow) and the dashboard (rose/amber/emerald) for the same P/C/F data — a coach learns two color languages. Rings rely on color as the sole signal (fails color-blind users and WCAG 2.2). No evidence of >=4.5:1 contrast audits in both themes, Dynamic Type, or screen-reader labels on the emoji/icon controls (satisfaction 1-3 emoji, portion 25/50/75/100). For a tool used one-handed at the eating moment, the 44/48px touch-target and thumb-reach floor is unverified.

topConcerns.title: The builder is two builders — split food UIs, split create paths, split save semantics
topConcerns.detail: Two food-browse UIs (FoodSearchDrawer vs FoodBrowser), two food-create flows (inline JSON vs standalone FormData), and a split-brain save model: grams saves atomically on Save, exchange portions auto-save on a 700ms debounce, cycles via a third action — so there is NO unified 'is this plan saved?' state. A coach cannot form a stable mental model of when their work is persisted. Exchanges (the paid 'metodo de los nutricionistas') only attach to client-plans, not templates, so the premium method can't be made reusable.

topConcerns.title: The premium pitch sells the artifact, not the workflow a nutritionist needs
topConcerns.detail: The paid module ships a tidy branded exchange PDF but no assessment->requirement->adequacy->monitoring loop, no allergy/intolerance enforcement (favorites only pin in search, never block an allergen), no insert-saved-meal/recipe on the canvas (rebuild food-by-food every time), and Edamam recipes live in a totally separate /coach/recipes surface never wired into the builder. The 4-macro ceiling (no fiber/sodium/micros) is also a UX ceiling: there is nowhere in any screen to show or target them.

recommendations.title: Build one canonical Nutrition surface per student and kill the other 8 entry points
recommendations.detail: Consolidate into a single Nutrition tab with a fixed top-of-fold summary (one adherence number, today's ring+bars, last-logged timestamp, risk flag) and progressive disclosure below (plan / history / analytics as sub-sections, not 12 stacked cards). Replace the buried 'Nutri. en riesgo' dot inside the training card and the suppressible top alert with a single deterministic nutrition status chip that always shows. Demote the dashboard hint and ClientStatsSheet tab to deep-links INTO this surface. Split the 1411-line NutritionTabB5 into edit-mode vs review-mode views.

recommendations.title: Define ONE adherence number, compute it once, surface it everywhere
recommendations.detail: Pick a single canonical formula (day-of-week-filtered % of prescribed meals over a rolling window) computed in one service, and feed the ring, strip, KPI, badge and heatmap from it. Remove the apologetic InfoTooltips. Where a second metric is genuinely useful (e.g. consistency = days logged), label it distinctly ('Consistencia 5/7 dias') and visually separate it from adherence. This is mostly a service-layer dedupe with a UI relabel — high trust payoff for medium effort.

recommendations.title: Relabel adherence vs intake honestly and adopt adherence-neutral framing
recommendations.detail: Rename the ring/bars context so coaches and clients see 'cumplimiento del plan' not implied real intake; add a one-line provenance note ('estimado desde el plan, no ingesta real'). Reframe streaks as restartable consistency ('5 de 7 dias') — never an all-or-nothing chain — and drop red over-budget shame styling. Add an opt-in 'comi algo distinto / fuera del plan' affordance so honest off-plan logging is possible instead of gamed completion. This is the single biggest retention + data-trust lever from 2026 best practice.

recommendations.title: Unify the macro design system and pass WCAG 2.2 AA in both themes
recommendations.detail: One P/C/F color token set shared across page, dashboard, PDF and mobile (define in the theme layer, not per-component). Pair every macro color with a text label and pattern so color is never the only signal. Audit contrast >=4.5:1 in light AND dark, set 44/48px minimum touch targets on portion/satisfaction/toggle controls, add aria-labels to all emoji/icon controls, and support Dynamic Type. Add a home-screen widget + lock-screen one-tap relog for the eating-moment capture trigger.

recommendations.title: Make the builder feel like one tool with one save model
recommendations.detail: Collapse to a single food-browse component and a single add-food flow; unify dirty/saved state into one visible indicator ('Guardado' / 'Cambios sin guardar') regardless of grams/exchanges/cycles underneath. Add an 'insertar comida guardada / receta' action on the canvas (wire Edamam recipes in here, retire the orphan /coach/recipes silo). Let exchange mode attach to templates so the premium method is reusable. Add a kcal cross-check banner (4P+4C+9F vs target) in BOTH modes, not just grams.

recommendations.title: Add multi-modal, low-friction logging on mobile (the unused native affordance)
recommendations.detail: expo-camera is installed but there is no barcode/photo affordance — the biggest gap vs every 2026 benchmark. Ship barcode-first (fastest, most accurate) against a verified DB, then AI-photo as a confirm/correct DRAFT with per-item confidence indicators and one-tap correction. Add recents/copy-meal and voice. Wire up the dead activeSwapMealIds so the alumno can actually apply coach swaps. Frame all of it as assistive draft, reconciled to one verified catalog.

recommendations.title: Add a structured client model in the builder: allergies, intolerances, dislikes
recommendations.detail: Promote client_food_preferences from 'pins favorites in search' to a structured allergy/intolerance/dislike layer that visibly BLOCKS or warns when an allergen is added to a plan, and powers allergy-aware swap suggestions. Surface it as a persistent panel in the builder so the coach always sees the client's constraints while authoring. This is the open wedge every PT platform misses and the floor for any real nutritionist.

recommendations.title: Surface the Chilean intercambios method as a first-class UI, not a hidden paid overlay
recommendations.detail: Present the 7-group porciones de intercambio as the primary authoring affordance for nutritionists (equivalence cards, one-tap macro-matched swaps), with the 'macros referenciales' provisional badge kept visible for honesty. This is how Chilean nutritionists actually prescribe and the clearest differentiator vs US-built apps — it deserves prominent IA, not a gated overlay only on client-plans.

recommendations.title: Give the coach a single review inbox for nutrition across all clients
recommendations.detail: A cross-client list ranked by nutrition risk (low adherence, empty plan, no log in N days) with timely-specific feedback affordances, so weekly review is one screen instead of hopping per-student across 9 surfaces. Reuse the same canonical adherence number. Pairs naturally with check-in as the glue linking nutrition + training + photos.

quickWins: Unify the P/C/F macro color tokens between page (orange/blue/yellow) and dashboard (rose/amber/emerald) — one shared theme token, immediate consistency win
Add aria-labels to the satisfaction emoji (1-3) and portion (25/50/75/100) controls and bump them to 44px touch targets
Remove the apologetic adherence InfoTooltips and replace with one labeled number once the canonical formula is chosen
Stop the nutrition top-alert from being silently suppressed by workout/checkin rules — show a persistent nutrition status chip instead
Relabel ring/bars as 'cumplimiento del plan (estimado)' so the UI stops implying real intake tracking
Reframe the streak copy as restartable consistency ('5 de 7 dias') and drop red over-budget shame coloring
Wire the existing /coach/recipes Edamam search into the builder as an 'insertar receta' action instead of leaving it as an orphan surface
Add a visible 'Guardado / cambios sin guardar' indicator to the builder so the debounce-vs-atomic save split is no longer invisible to the coach

roleFindings.role: Head of Sales (B2B Enterprise) — gyms, clinics, and nutritionist-led practices in Chile/Latam
roleFindings.verdict: Sellable today as a white-label coach tool with a slick branded exchange-PDF, but it loses every deal where a licensed nutritionist is in the room — the 4-macro ceiling, fake 'intake' numbers, and a food DB the RD can't cite are three demo-killing objections we cannot currently answer.

topConcerns.title: 4-macro ceiling is a hard clinical wall — disqualifies us from any clinic RFP
topConcerns.detail: Only kcal/P/C/F exist anywhere (foods, goals, logging, PDF, alerts). No fiber/sodium/sugar/sat-fat/micros means we literally cannot manage hypertension, renal, diabetes, prenatal, or deficiency cases. A nutritionist asks 'dónde veo el sodio?' in the first 90 seconds of a demo and we have no answer. This single gap caps every nutritionist-led account.

topConcerns.title: 'Consumed' macros are plan-adherence, not real intake — misleading-claims and trust risk
topConcerns.detail: Rings can read 100% while the client ate something completely different; the number is planned-macros × completion%. There's no off-plan/free-food entry and the streak/heatmap reward marking-done, so the metric is gameable. We are selling 'tracking' UI that implies real intake we don't capture — a credibility and arguably a consumer-protection exposure if a clinic relies on it.

topConcerns.title: Food data is coach-typed, INTEGER-rounded, and un-citable — no provenance an RD will defend
topConcerns.detail: 343 foods, no source/verified flag, no INTA/USDA/national table or barcode in the builder, Edamam stranded in a separate recipes page. A licensed nutritionist cannot put numbers she can't cite into a clinical report. This is the data-credibility objection that ends bake-offs against Cronometer/Fitia/Nutrium.

topConcerns.title: Adherence has 2 formulas and 4 definitions shown at once — the demo contradicts itself on screen
topConcerns.detail: client-detail counts all meal logs (no day-of-week filter); dashboard filters by day-of-week, so the Overview ring, Nutrición strip, KPI, and today badge can disagree for the same student on the same screen, papered over with apologetic tooltips. A buyer who spots two different adherence numbers stops trusting the whole product.

topConcerns.title: Org/team bulk-assign is destructive, non-selective, and transaction-less — it can wipe a gym's plans mid-rollout
topConcerns.detail: assignOrgNutritionPlanTemplate always targets ALL active org clients (UI implies a subset), unconditionally deactivates prior plans, inserts macro-only plans with no meals, and has no transaction — a partial failure leaves clients with zero active plan. This is exactly the multi-coach workflow we pitch to gyms/clinics; one bad rollout is a reference-account-ending incident.

topConcerns.title: The paid 'método de los nutricionistas' pitch sells the artifact, not the reasoning
topConcerns.detail: We charge a $9.990 module and pitch the dietitian method, but deliver a tidy branded exchange PDF — not assessment → requirement calc → adequacy → monitoring. Nutrition is even siloed from the separate body_composition add-on, so targets can't consume ISAK/BIA. A real RD sees through this fast; it threatens the premium positioning and the add-on price.

topConcerns.title: Mobile lacks barcode/photo logging despite expo-camera installed — we miss the 2026 demo moment
topConcerns.detail: The single biggest unused native affordance. Competitors win coach deals on ~2s photo-to-macros and barcode scan; our alumno can't even apply coach swaps (dead-wired) and the entire exchanges differentiator is absent on mobile, where clients actually log.

topConcerns.title: Scope-column UPDATE grants on nutrition_plans and foods — an infosec review will flag cross-tenant exposure
topConcerns.detail: client_id/coach_id/org_id are UPDATE-grantable to authenticated (gated only by RLS WITH CHECK), and foods has no column allowlist, so a coach could flip coach_id->NULL to push a private food toward the global pool. Contradicts our own clients-hardening doctrine and would stall an enterprise procurement security gate.

recommendations.title: Ship a verified, Chile-layered, source-tagged food catalog with barcode
recommendations.detail: Ingest INTA/SAFOODS (Chilean foods, manual from the 2018 table + Schmidt-Hebbel) + FatSecret Platform API (Latam brands/barcodes, free tier) + Open Food Facts (free barcode) + USDA fallback. Tag every entry with source DB + verified flag, rank verified/local first, store decimals not INTEGER. Wire into the builder AND mobile food search. Converts 'your numbers are guesses' into 'cite-able, local, defensible' — the highest-leverage sales asset.

recommendations.title: Add the micronutrient layer (start with the clinical-critical subset)
recommendations.detail: Extend foods/goals/logging/PDF with fiber, sodium, sugar, sat/unsat fat first (covers hypertension/diabetes/cardiac), then iron/calcium/potassium/folate for renal/prenatal. Even a 'top-8 nutrients' release unblocks the clinical buyer and lets sales say 'sí, maneja casos clínicos'. Removes the dominant deal-killer.

recommendations.title: Unify adherence to ONE source-of-truth and label intake honestly
recommendations.detail: Collapse to a single day-of-week-filtered formula in a shared service consumed by every surface. Relabel 'consumido' as 'cumplimiento del plan' everywhere, and add a real off-plan/free-food log path (text + portion >100%) to graduate toward true intake. Kills both the on-screen contradiction and the misleading-claims exposure.

recommendations.title: Build mobile barcode + photo-draft logging on the installed expo-camera
recommendations.detail: Barcode scan against the verified DB plus AI photo as a confirm/correct draft with per-item confidence. The demo moment that wins coach buyers in 2026 and closes our biggest native gap. Bring the exchanges module and coach-swap application to mobile in the same push so our differentiator exists where clients log.

recommendations.title: Make org/team assignment selective, non-destructive, and transactional
recommendations.detail: Honor the subset the UI shows, never blanket-deactivate, propagate meals (kill the dead org_nutrition_templates path or make it real), wrap in a transaction with per-row error isolation, and batch the serial propagateTemplateChanges loop. Add an idempotent empty-meal/empty-plan guard in CI. Protects the exact multi-coach workflow we sell to gyms and clinics.

recommendations.title: Wire assessment -> targets and add safety cross-checks
recommendations.detail: Connect the body_composition add-on into nutrition so targets auto-derive from ISAK/BIA (Mifflin default, Cunningham/Katch-McArdle when LBM exists), and add 4P+4C+9F≈kcal validation plus protein-floor and high/low kcal bounds. Gives the nutritionist the assessment→requirement→plan reasoning chain, not just the artifact.

recommendations.title: Add allergy/intolerance enforcement and condition templates
recommendations.detail: Make client_food_preferences block allergens in search/swap/auto-plan, and ship condition templates (diabetes, CKD, PCOS, low-FODMAP, cardiac) plus exchange-based TEMPLATES (today exchanges are client-plan-only). These are the bake-off checkboxes that let us claim the open wedge: white-label intercambios + allergy filters serving coaches AND nutritionists in one app.

recommendations.title: Harden tenant scoping grants on nutrition_plans and foods
recommendations.detail: Revoke UPDATE on scope columns (client_id/coach_id/org_id) from authenticated and grant a column allowlist, matching the clients-hardening doctrine — foods has no allowlist today. Clears the infosec-review objection before it stalls an enterprise procurement gate.

quickWins: Relabel every 'consumido'/'consumed' macro surface as 'cumplimiento del plan' so we stop implying real intake — removes the misleading-claims objection with zero schema change.
Pick ONE adherence definition (day-of-week-filtered) and route all four surfaces through the existing shared service; delete the apologetic InfoTooltips.
Add the kcal cross-check (4P+4C+9F vs daily_calories) and an empty-meal/empty-plan save guard — both small, both demo-able as 'safety rails'.
Revoke the scope-column UPDATE grants on nutrition_plans and foods (column allowlist) to clear the infosec-review objection cheaply.
Surface barcode scan on mobile against the current DB even before the verified catalog lands — expo-camera is already installed; it's the visible 2026 demo moment.
Promote /coach/recipes (Edamam) into the plan builder so the 'insert a real food/recipe' story exists in one place instead of a stranded surface.

roleFindings.role: SDR (Sales Development Representative) — outbound to Chilean/Latam coaches and nutritionists
roleFindings.verdict: EVA today sells a beautiful branded "PDF de intercambios" that wins coaches but gets disqualified in a real nutricionista's first three questions (Chilean food data, micros, intake truth) — the hook opens the wrong door.

topConcerns.title: No one-liner hook survives a nutritionist's first 10 seconds
topConcerns.detail: My openers must lead with one sharp claim. The only defensible hook today is 'el metodo de los intercambios, blanco con tu marca' — it lands with coaches, but a nutricionista instantly asks: de donde salen los datos de los alimentos? tienen fibra/sodio/micros? usan tabla INTA? We have nothing. The 4-macro ceiling, coach-typed INTEGER-rounded foods, and 0 org foods mean the demo collapses under any clinical question. I need a hook that opens the conversation instead of one that gets the call disqualified.

topConcerns.title: Adherence numbers that don't reconcile kill trust in the demo
topConcerns.detail: Two divergent adherence formulas and four definitions show simultaneously (heatmap=logged a meal, strip=% of plan meals, ring=weekly avg %, badge=today %). When a prospect opens two screens in a trial and sees different adherence for the same client, the deal stalls on 'esto no me cuadra.' A sales motion cannot survive a product that argues with itself on its headline metric.

topConcerns.title: 'Consumed' = plan-adherence, not real intake — a liability in the pitch
topConcerns.detail: calculateConsumedMacrosWithCompletionFallback credits full planned macros on tap-to-complete. Rings read 100% while the client ate something else entirely; streaks reward marking-done. If I oversell this as 'tracking de macros' to a knowledgeable coach, the first power-user catches it and the trust is gone. I either need real intake/off-plan logging or a brutally honest 'adherencia al plan' label I can sell without overpromising.

topConcerns.title: Chile/Latam-native is our wedge but the data layer doesn't back it
topConcerns.detail: Our strongest differentiator vs US-built apps (Trainerize, That Clean Life) is the Chilean porciones-de-intercambio method AND local food data. We have the method but no INTA/SAFOODS, no FatSecret Latam, no barcode in the builder, no verification/source flag. Competitors leave the dietitian wedge wide open; we can own it, but right now I'm selling a promise the catalog can't keep.

topConcerns.title: Org/team bulk-assign is destructive and demo-fragile
topConcerns.detail: assignOrgNutritionPlanTemplateToClientsAction hits ALL active org clients, deactivates prior plans, inserts macro-only mealless plans, no transaction. For a 300-client Movida-style account this is exactly the buyer who needs reliability most, and a partial failure leaves clients with zero active plan. This is a churn-and-refund landmine I'd be selling into the highest-ACV segment.

recommendations.title: Reframe the hook to what we actually own: 'intercambios chilenos, blanco con tu marca, con la app del alumno incluida'
recommendations.detail: Lead outbound with the one true wedge no competitor in Chile/Latam fills: native Chilean 7-group porciones de intercambio + white-label PDF + included client app/PWA + offline 1-tap logging. This is real today and differentiates vs MyFitnessPal/Trainerize. Target message: 'deja de armar planes en Excel y mandar PDFs sin marca — EVA te da intercambios, tu marca y la app del alumno en uno.' Sell the artifact + daily loop we genuinely win, not clinical depth we don't.

recommendations.title: Ship a source-tagged, verified Chilean food catalog (INTA/SAFOODS + FatSecret Latam + barcode)
recommendations.detail: This is the single biggest deal-unlocker for nutritionists. Layer INTA/SAFOODS (manual ingest, Chilean foods) + FatSecret Platform API (Latam brands/barcodes) + Open Food Facts (free barcode first-pass) + USDA fallback. Add a source + 'verificado' flag, rank verified/local first, kill INTEGER rounding. Gives me a defensible answer to 'de donde salen los datos' and turns the demo from typed-by-coach into citable. Foundational to every nutritionist pitch.

recommendations.title: Collapse adherence to one definition with one label
recommendations.detail: Pick a single adherence formula (day-of-week filtered, % of prescribed meals), use it everywhere, and label consumed numbers explicitly as 'adherencia al plan' not intake. Remove the apologetic InfoTooltips. A reconciled headline metric is what lets a trial convert without the 'no me cuadra' objection. Low effort, removes my #2 deal-staller.

recommendations.title: Add full macro panel (fiber, sodium, sugar, sat fat) + a kcal cross-check + allergy/intolerance enforcement
recommendations.detail: You don't need 30 micros to open the nutritionist segment — fiber/sodium/sugar/saturated fat plus a 4P+4C+9F≈kcal sanity check plus structured allergy filters (block allergens in swaps, not just pin favorites) clears the credibility floor for diabetes/hypertension/general clinical work. This is the difference between 'macro tool para coaches' and 'sirve para nutricionistas de verdad' in my pitch.

recommendations.title: Make org/team bulk-assign selective, mealful, and transactional before pitching big accounts
recommendations.detail: Fix assignOrgNutritionPlanTemplateToClientsAction to target a chosen subset, propagate the rich org template's meals (not macro-only), and wrap in a transaction with per-row error isolation. This de-risks the highest-ACV Movida-style sale where a botched rollout = refund + reputation hit in a tight Chilean coach community.

recommendations.title: Wire the body_composition add-on into nutrition targets (assessment → TDEE → plan)
recommendations.detail: Let nutrition consume ISAK/BIA lean-mass from the body_composition module: Mifflin default, switch to Cunningham/Katch-McArdle when LBM is captured. This is a natural upsell story ('compra los dos modulos, trabajan juntos') and gives me a reason-to-buy for the second paid module instead of two siloed $9.990 features.

recommendations.title: Activate expo-camera for barcode/photo logging as the 2026 frontier line
recommendations.detail: expo-camera is already installed but unused. Even barcode-only (no AI) against the new catalog is a concrete 'log con la camara' demo beat that matches Cal AI/MacroSnap expectations and makes the alumno app feel modern in a 30-second screen-share. AI photo can come later as assistive draft; barcode is the cheap, accurate, sellable first step.

quickWins: Relabel every 'consumido/consumed' surface as 'adherencia al plan' so the pitch stops overpromising real intake tracking — removes my biggest credibility risk for free.
Lead outbound with 'intercambios chilenos + tu marca + app del alumno incluida' (the wedge we actually own) instead of generic 'metodo de nutricionistas.'
Standardize macro ring colors across page/dashboard/mobile (orange/blue/yellow vs rose/amber/emerald) — a polished demo where the same number is the same color builds trust at zero clinical cost.
Add a verification/source badge slot in the food UI even before the full catalog lands, so 'verificado / tabla chilena' is visible the moment data improves.
Pick ONE adherence number for the dashboard KPI and the client ring so trials don't surface contradictory figures mid-call.

roleFindings.verdict: EVA's nutrition system is an adoption trap for the exact persona it's being sold to: it onboards a personal-trainer fine but actively repels a licensed nutritionist (no verified food DB, no micros, intake-vs-adherence confusion, numbers that don't reconcile) — and the fragmentation across 9 review surfaces guarantees a heavy, recurring support load that no amount of CS hand-holding can offset.

topConcerns.title: Adherence shows 4 contradictory numbers for the same student — the #1 future support ticket
topConcerns.detail: Two divergent formulas (client-detail.service counts all meal logs with no day-of-week filter; dashboard.service filters by day) feed an Overview ring, a Nutricion strip, a dashboard KPI, and a today badge that can legitimately disagree. The product already ships apologetic InfoTooltips to explain the discrepancy. Every nutritionist who cross-checks two screens files a 'your data is wrong' ticket, and CS cannot win that argument because both numbers are 'correct' under different definitions. This single issue will dominate support volume and torch trust in week one of any real nutritionist's trial. It must be one number, one definition, everywhere, before onboarding a single paid nutritionist.

topConcerns.title: The paid pitch ('el metodo de los nutricionistas') oversells what the tool can clinically do
topConcerns.detail: 4-macro ceiling (kcal/P/C/F only — zero fiber, sodium, sugar, micronutrients), coach-typed INTEGER-rounded food data with no source/verified flag, no allergy enforcement (favorites only, never blocks an allergen), and 'consumed' macros that are plan-adherence not real intake. A real RD hits the clinical wall in the first consult (hypertension/renal/diabetes/prenatal impossible) and churns — and feels misled, which is a refund/reputation risk in a small Chilean market where nutritionists talk to each other. CS is the one who eats that gap. The marketing claim and the product capability must be reconciled: either narrow the pitch to 'coach macro + intercambios tool' now, or fund the clinical floor before selling to RDs.

topConcerns.title: Nutrition has no canonical home — review is a 9-surface scavenger hunt
topConcerns.detail: To do one weekly review a coach hops across Overview ring + a red dot buried in the TRAINING card + a top alert that is silently suppressed by workout/checkin rules + a 1411-line Nutricion mega-tab (12 cards) + charts hidden under a body-composition toggle in Progreso + the separate builder + the separate /coach/recipes surface. This is the dominant adoption blocker: time-to-value is terrible, the 'aha' is buried, and onboarding/training material balloons because there's no single flow to teach. Fragmentation directly inflates onboarding time and support load.

topConcerns.title: Silent data corruption + destructive bulk-assign will produce 'EVA broke my client's plan' incidents
topConcerns.detail: An April 2026 manual audit (not in CI/cron) found 17 empty meals across 6 clients and 2 clients with entirely empty plans still accruing daily logs — nothing prevents saving an empty meal or activating an empty plan, and these export to branded PDF as valid. Separately, org bulk-assign always targets ALL active clients (UI implies a subset), unconditionally deactivates prior plans, inserts macro-only plans wiping customization, with no transaction (partial failure = clients left with zero active plan). For a 300-client team (Movida-scale), one bad run is a mass incident and a trust-ending support escalation.

topConcerns.title: Food edits and positional meal-keying rewrite history retroactively
topConcerns.detail: Macros read live from foods at calc time, so editing a food silently changes every past log's computed intake — a nutritionist fixing a typo unknowingly invalidates weeks of adherence analytics, and can't reproduce what a client 'ate'. Grams-mode reconciliation keys on order_index, so reorder+save can attach a client's historical logs to the wrong meal (the exchanges path was fixed; grams wasn't). Both manifest as confusing, hard-to-diagnose CS tickets ('the numbers changed by themselves') with no good answer.

topConcerns.title: Mobile parity gaps undercut the alumno daily loop that drives retention
topConcerns.detail: On mobile the alumno cannot apply coach swaps (data fetched but dead-wired), the entire exchanges module is absent, there's no off-plan logging, historical days show live not snapshotted targets, and expo-camera is installed with no barcode/photo affordance. The alumno-facing daily loop is the retention engine; gaps here mean coaches get blamed by their clients and escalate to EVA. The single biggest unused lever (camera/barcode) is exactly the 2026 table-stakes capture mechanism.

recommendations.title: Collapse adherence to ONE definition and ONE number, surfaced identically everywhere
recommendations.detail: Pick a single canonical adherence formula (day-of-week-filtered % of prescribed meals, weekly-averaged), implement it once in a shared service, and render the same value on dashboard KPI, Overview ring, Nutricion strip, and today badge. Delete the divergent client-detail count and the apologetic InfoTooltips. Label it honestly as 'cumplimiento del plan' (plan adherence), NOT 'consumido/intake', so no nutritionist mistakes it for real nutrient tracking. This kills the top future support ticket and is the cheapest trust win available.

recommendations.title: Build a single canonical Nutrition review home and demote the other 8 surfaces to links into it
recommendations.detail: Make the Nutricion tab the one place a coach reviews and edits nutrition; reduce the dashboard/overview/progreso appearances to a single consistent KPI that deep-links into it. Break the 1411-line NutritionTabB5 into review (read) vs edit (builder link) so the daily 'aha' (is my client on track, what do I fix) is reachable in one tap. This is the highest-leverage onboarding/time-to-value fix and directly shrinks training material and support volume.

recommendations.title: Ship the clinical credibility floor before selling to real nutritionists: verified source-tagged food DB + barcode + micros
recommendations.detail: Layer a region-appropriate catalog (INTA/SAFOODS for Chilean foods, FatSecret for Latam brands/barcodes, USDA fallback), tag every entry with source + verification flag, rank verified/local first, and extend foods beyond 4 macros to at least fiber/sodium/sugar/sat-fat plus a micro panel. Wire barcode/photo into the builder AND mobile food search (expo-camera already installed). Without this, no licensed RD takes EVA seriously; with it, EVA clears the wedge every PT platform leaves open in Chile/Latam.

recommendations.title: Add guardrails that prevent the silent-corruption and destructive-assign incidents
recommendations.detail: Block saving an empty meal and activating an empty plan (validation + a kcal cross-check 4P+4C+9F approx kcal, protein floor, kcal<1200/high bound). Make org bulk-assign selective (honor the subset the UI implies), wrap propagation in a transaction with Promise.all and per-row error isolation, and move the empty-plan audit into a cron with alerting. Snapshot food macros per-log so edits don't rewrite history; key grams reconciliation by id like the exchanges path. These convert future mass-incident escalations into prevented non-events.

recommendations.title: Make adherence-neutral, allergy-aware UX the product default (retention lever)
recommendations.detail: Replace all-or-nothing streaks with restartable 'logged 5 of 7 days' framing, remove punishing red over-budget styling, unify the divergent macro color palettes across web/dashboard/mobile, and add structured allergy/intolerance/dislike fields that actually block an allergen in swaps and search (not just pin favorites). Fire reminders at the client's own habitual mealtimes, only when something is prescribed today. This protects the alumno daily loop that drives coach retention and reduces 'my client stopped logging' escalations.

recommendations.title: Close mobile parity on the alumno loop: coach swaps, snapshotted history, off-plan logging, capture affordance
recommendations.detail: Wire activeSwapMealIds so the alumno can apply coach swaps on mobile, read the target_*_at_log snapshots already written so historical days show correct targets, allow a labeled off-plan/free-food entry, and add a photo/barcode capture button. This makes the client-facing experience match what coaches were sold and removes a recurring source of coach-blames-EVA tickets.

recommendations.title: Unify the template stores and food-create paths to cut onboarding confusion and drift
recommendations.detail: Three template tables (two competing org ones, one dead-end, one mealless, plus an unsurfaced team pool) and two food-create + two food-browse UIs mean CS cannot give one clean 'here's how you build and reuse a plan' answer. Consolidate to one template model that carries meals, one food-create flow, one browser, and allow exchange-based templates (today exchanges are client-plan-only). Fewer paths = shorter onboarding, less drift, fewer 'why did my template not carry over' tickets.

quickWins: Re-label every 'consumido/macros' UI string that is actually plan-adherence as 'cumplimiento del plan' so no nutritionist mistakes completion% for real intake — pure copy change, removes a credibility landmine.
Add a hard validation blocking save of an empty meal and activation of an empty plan — stops the documented data-corruption class at the source.
Promote the existing audit-nutrition-empty-meals.mjs script into a scheduled cron with an alert so corruption is caught proactively instead of by a manual one-off.
Unify the macro ring color palette across page, dashboard, and mobile (orange/blue/yellow vs rose/amber/emerald) — small CSS/token fix that ends 'the colors mean different things' confusion.
Make org bulk-assign honor the subset the UI implies (stop silently targeting ALL active clients) — a scoping fix that prevents accidental mass plan-wipes.
Add a kcal sanity cross-check (4P+4C+9F approx daily_calories) banner in BOTH grams and exchange modes (today the 5% banner is grams-only) so prescribed plans can't silently miss their stated goal.

roleFindings.role: Legal & Compliance Counsel (Chile) — health/nutrition data, Ley 19.628 / Ley 21.719, medical-scope & SERNAC billing
roleFindings.verdict: EVA processes health data (food intake, body composition, adherence) at scale with no consent layer, no data-category classification, no DPA-grade controls, and re-scopable tenant columns — it is not compliant with Ley 21.719 (effective Dec 2026) and carries real medical-scope and SERNAC exposure today.

topConcerns.title: Health data processed with no consent layer (Ley 21.719 'datos sensibles')
topConcerns.detail: Food intake, adherence, satisfaction scores, body-composition/ISAK and clinical-adjacent prescriptions are 'datos sensibles' / 'datos relativos a la salud' under Ley 21.719 (reform of 19.628, in force Dec 2026, fines up to ~UTM 5.000 / ~10% revenue). EVA has NO explicit, granular, purpose-bound consent capture for the alumno before a coach builds and stores a nutrition plan, NO consent record/timestamp/version, and NO lawful-basis classification of these tables as sensitive. The coach-alumno relationship is treated as implicit authorization — it is not. P0 because the law is dated and the data category is the most heavily regulated one.

topConcerns.title: Cross-tenant re-scoping grant gap exposes health data (RLS-only, contrary to clients-hardening doctrine)
topConcerns.detail: client_id/coach_id/org_id on nutrition_plans and foods are UPDATE-grantable to authenticated, gated only by RLS WITH CHECK and not by column-grant revocation (the CLAUDE.md clients doctrine explicitly hardened this everywhere else). A logic slip in any policy = one coach's client health plan re-scoped to another tenant = an unauthorized disclosure of sensitive data, i.e. a reportable breach under 21.719's new breach-notification duty. foods has no allowlist at all (coach_id->NULL promotes a private food to global). This is the single highest-likelihood path to a sensitive-data leak.

topConcerns.title: Medical-advice / professional-scope exposure (nutritionist vs coach, no scope gating)
topConcerns.detail: The product is pitched as 'el metodo de los nutricionistas' and a coach with NO nutrition credential can build and prescribe calorie/macro plans to clients, including weight-loss plans, with a hardcoded kcal<1200 alert as the ONLY safety rail (no high-end bound, no protein floor, no kcal cross-check). In Chile, nutritional prescription is a regulated health act; EVA enables unlicensed users to perform it under a clinical-sounding brand. No captured professional-credential field, no scope-of-practice gating, no per-plan medical disclaimer at point of prescription/consumption (only the PDF carries a disclaimer). Exposure: SERNAC (misleading 'metodo de nutricionistas'), and contributory liability if an unqualified coach causes harm.

topConcerns.title: No data-subject rights machinery (access, deletion, portability, rectification)
topConcerns.detail: Ley 21.719 grants ARCOP rights (acceso, rectificacion, cancelacion, oposicion, portabilidad). EVA has no alumno-facing export/delete of their own nutrition+health data, no defined retention period for daily_nutrition_logs / nutrition_plan_history snapshots / body-composition data, and history snapshots persist indefinitely with no purge. A deletion request today cannot be honored cleanly because nutrition data is split-brain across 15 tables + jsonb snapshots + mobile AsyncStorage caches.

topConcerns.title: SERNAC billing-evidence gap for the paid nutrition add-on
topConcerns.detail: Nutrition is sold as a paid module ($9.990) and via team contracts. billing_snapshots captures the charge breakdown, BUT the value delivered is the artifact (a branded PDF), not a defensible service: adherence has 2 divergent formulas and 4 incompatible definitions shown simultaneously, 'consumed' macros are plan-adherence not real intake (rings can read 100% while the client ate differently), and empty/empty-plan clients still accrue logs and export as valid PDFs. If a consumer disputes 'que me cobraron y que recibi', the delivered evidence is internally contradictory — weak SERNAC posture.

topConcerns.title: Third-party data flows (Edamam recipes, future FatSecret/AI photo) with no DPA / transfer basis
topConcerns.detail: Recipe search already calls Edamam; the roadmap adds FatSecret, Open Food Facts, AI photo analysis. Sending alumno meal/intake data to foreign processors triggers Ley 21.719 international-transfer rules and requires a written processing agreement (encargado de tratamiento) + adequacy/standard-clauses basis. No DPA, no sub-processor register, no data-minimization gate before calling third parties exists today.

topConcerns.title: Org bulk-assign destroys data with no audit trail or rollback
topConcerns.detail: assignOrgNutritionPlanTemplateToClientsAction unconditionally deactivates ALL active plans for ALL org clients, transaction-less, macro-only — partial failure leaves clients with zero active plan and no record of what was overwritten. From a compliance lens this is uncontrolled mutation of health records with no immutable audit log (org_audit_logs is not wired here), undermining both accountability (21.719) and any dispute reconstruction.

topConcerns.title: Retroactive food edits rewrite historical health records
topConcerns.detail: Macros are read live from foods at calc time; editing a food silently rewrites every past log's computed intake. Health records must be tamper-evident and reproducible. Non-reproducible adherence analytics are both a clinical-integrity problem and a legal-evidence problem (you cannot attest to what a client was shown on a given date).

recommendations.title: Add explicit, versioned health-data consent at plan assignment
recommendations.detail: Before the first nutrition plan is stored/shown to an alumno, capture granular consent: purpose (nutritional guidance), data categories (intake, body composition, adherence), processor disclosures (Edamam/FatSecret/AI), with consent_version, timestamp, IP, and withdrawal path persisted in a dedicated consent table. Block plan visibility until consent recorded. This is the keystone Ley 21.719 control.

recommendations.title: Revoke column UPDATE grants on nutrition_plans.scope + foods, mirror clients doctrine
recommendations.detail: REVOKE UPDATE ON nutrition_plans / foods FROM authenticated; GRANT UPDATE on an explicit allowlist that EXCLUDES client_id/coach_id/org_id (service-role-only re-scoping). Add foods column allowlist so coach_id cannot be nulled. Extend tests/separation/module-grants.sql to assert no drift on these tables. Closes the highest-likelihood sensitive-data leak path.

recommendations.title: Gate nutritional prescription on professional scope + point-of-use disclaimers
recommendations.detail: Add a coach professional-credential field (nutricionista colegiado vs coach) and a scope flag. For non-credentialed coaches, surface an explicit in-app disclaimer at plan-build AND at alumno-consumption ('orientacion general, no constituye prescripcion nutricional ni reemplaza atencion profesional'), plus mandatory safety bounds (kcal floor+ceiling, protein floor, 4P+4C+9F kcal cross-check). Stamp the disclaimer into every plan render, not only the PDF.

recommendations.title: Fix or qualify the 'metodo de los nutricionistas' marketing claim
recommendations.detail: Either substantiate the claim (credentialed-nutritionist-built templates, intercambios per the Chilean Manual de Porciones de Intercambio 2021, RD-validated recipe library) or soften it to avoid SERNAC misleading-advertising exposure. Keep dated evidence of substantiation on file. Cheap and removes a concrete consumer-law risk.

recommendations.title: Build ARCOP data-subject rights endpoints for alumno health data
recommendations.detail: One-tap alumno export (their nutrition+adherence+body-comp data) and a delete/anonymize flow that sweeps all 15 tables + jsonb snapshots + mobile caches. Define and enforce a retention policy (e.g. logs N months, history snapshots N months then anonymize). Required for 21.719 ARCOP + breach-readiness.

recommendations.title: Sign DPAs and build a sub-processor register + transfer basis before any third-party food/AI call
recommendations.detail: Written encargado-de-tratamiento agreements with Edamam and any FatSecret/Open Food Facts/AI-photo vendor; document international-transfer basis; minimize payload (send food strings, never client identifiers) and log what leaves. Add a sub-processor list to the privacy policy.

recommendations.title: Unify adherence to ONE defined, documented formula and label consumed-as-adherence
recommendations.detail: Pick a single adherence definition (day-of-week-filtered % of prescribed meals completed), apply it in client-detail.service AND dashboard.service, and remove the apologetic InfoTooltips. Label 'consumido' explicitly as 'adherencia al plan (estimado), no ingesta real' everywhere. Directly strengthens the SERNAC value-delivered story and clinical trust.

recommendations.title: Make health records tamper-evident: snapshot food macros per log; immutable audit log on bulk ops
recommendations.detail: Snapshot the food's macro values into the log row at log time (stop reading live foods for historical calc) so past intake is reproducible. Wire org bulk-assign and propagation through org_audit_logs (who/what/when/prior-state) inside a DB transaction with per-row error isolation. Gives reproducible, defensible records for disputes and 21.719 accountability.

recommendations.title: Classify nutrition/body-comp tables as sensitive and document the processing record (RAT)
recommendations.detail: Maintain a Registro de Actividades de Tratamiento listing these tables as 'datos de salud', lawful basis (consent), retention, recipients/processors, and security measures. Tag the tables in schema docs. This is the documentation 21.719 expects an accountable controller to hold.

quickWins: REVOKE column UPDATE on nutrition_plans scope columns + foods from authenticated (one migration, mirrors existing clients doctrine).
Soften or substantiate the 'metodo de los nutricionistas' copy to cut SERNAC misleading-advertising risk.
Stamp the existing PDF legal disclaimer into every in-app plan render (build + consumption), not just exports.
Add kcal floor+ceiling, protein-floor, and 4P+4C+9F kcal cross-check validations as hard safety rails.
Block activation/save of empty meals and empty plans so contradictory PDFs stop reaching consumers (SERNAC evidence hygiene).
Add a professional-credential field to coach profile now (data capture) even before scope-gating UI ships.

roleFindings.verdict: EVA's nutrition is a self-hosted, coach-typed macro tool with zero external food-data integrations and a billing layer that is structurally sound but sells the artifact (branded PDF), not citable data, capping it below clinical-grade for Chile/Latam nutritionists.

topConcerns.title: Zero external food-data integration: catalog is coach-typed, unverifiable, legally indefensible
topConcerns.detail: 343 rows, all manually entered, INTEGER-rounded, 4 macros, no source/provenance/verification column. A licensed nutritionist cannot cite a value in a clinical report, and EVA carries silent liability if a coach mistypes a macro on a renal/diabetic plan. No USDA FDC, FatSecret, Open Food Facts, or INTA/SAFOODS pipeline exists in the builder or mobile. Edamam is quarantined in /coach/recipes and never feeds food_items. Every downstream surface (logging, adherence, PDF, alerts) inherits unverifiable data. This is the dominant integration gap.

topConcerns.title: No barcode capture despite expo-camera already installed
topConcerns.detail: expo-camera is in the mobile bundle with no barcode/photo affordance wired. Barcode is the fastest, most accurate capture mode and the single biggest unused native affordance. Without a barcode -> verified-DB lookup (Open Food Facts free first-pass, FatSecret for Latam brands/supermarket SKUs), the alumno can only check off a pre-planned meal. This blocks the entire multi-modal logging bar (photo/voice/barcode/text) that 2026 consumer apps treat as table stakes.

topConcerns.title: Consumed = plan-adherence, not real intake: rings can read 100% while the client ate something else
topConcerns.detail: calculateConsumedMacrosWithCompletionFallback credits full planned macros on completion; there is no off-plan/free-food entry, no >100% portion, no barcode-logged food. Streak/heatmap reward marking-done, so the data model is gameable and can reinforce dishonest self-report. From a data-licensing/billing-integrity view, EVA charges for 'nutrition tracking' while the UI implies real macro tracking it does not perform. This conflation must be resolved before any wearable/CGM/Apple Health intake import has meaning.

topConcerns.title: Scope columns on nutrition_plans and foods are UPDATE-grantable to authenticated
topConcerns.detail: client_id/coach_id/org_id are gated only by RLS WITH CHECK, contrary to the clients-hardening column-grant doctrine in CLAUDE.md. foods has no column allowlist, so a coach could flip coach_id->NULL to promote a private food toward the global catalog -- a cross-tenant data-poisoning vector that becomes critical once an external/verified catalog coexists with coach entries. Harden grants before integrating any 3rd-party food source so external rows cannot be re-scoped or overwritten.

topConcerns.title: Add-on billing sells the PDF artifact, not the data; no source-DB licensing accounted for
topConcerns.detail: The $9.990 nutrition module and the separate body_composition module are siloed, so targets cannot consume ISAK/BIA lean-mass for a real TDEE engine, and the exchanges 'metodo de nutricionistas' pitch monetizes a branded exchange PDF rather than verified reasoning. When a paid food DB (FatSecret) enters, its per-seat/redistribution licensing terms must map onto coach_addons entitlements -- today there is no field, cost model, or attribution surface for 3rd-party data licensing, which will block a clean commercial integration.

topConcerns.title: Two parallel nutrition gates with no unifying resolver; cross-dependency hand-enforced
topConcerns.detail: tier canUseNutrition inline in RSC vs enabled_modules/coach_addons via assertModule, with the nutrition<->body_composition cross-dependency enforced in only 2-3 call sites. Any future integration surface (barcode endpoint, wearable webhook, food-import API) can check one gate and forget the other, leaking a paid capability. A single entitlement resolver is a prerequisite to safely add billable integration endpoints.

recommendations.title: Add source + verification provenance to foods, then layer a region-tiered catalog
recommendations.detail: Add source_db (inta_safoods|fatsecret|open_food_facts|usda_fdc|coach_custom), source_id, verified bool, and last_synced_at to foods; convert macros to NUMERIC. Ingest INTA/SAFOODS + Schmidt-Hebbel as the Chilean verified core (manual/ETL, no API), FatSecret Platform API for Latam brands/barcodes, Open Food Facts for free barcode first-pass, USDA FDC as generic fallback. Rank verified/local first in FoodSearchDrawer and FoodBrowser; badge coach-custom as unverified. This is the credibility floor for nutritionists.

recommendations.title: Wire barcode + verified-DB lookup into mobile food search using the installed expo-camera
recommendations.detail: Ship barcode scan -> Open Food Facts (free) -> FatSecret (Latam SKUs) resolution returning a source-tagged food row. This unlocks real off-plan logging and is the fastest path to multi-modal capture. Reconcile every input mode against the single verified foods table. Gate behind the nutrition entitlement via the unified resolver below.

recommendations.title: Introduce a real-intake logging path distinct from plan-adherence
recommendations.detail: Add an off-plan/free-food log writing actual consumed grams + a per-log macro snapshot (fixes the retroactive food-edit history-rewrite too). Keep the existing tap-to-complete as 'adherence' but relabel it as plan-adherence in the UI, and separate 'meets target' (real intake) from 'followed plan'. This makes wearable/CGM/Apple Health import meaningful and de-games the streak.

recommendations.title: Build one entitlement resolver for nutrition (tier + addon + cross-dependency)
recommendations.detail: Replace the inline canUseNutrition and scattered assertModule calls with a single canUseNutrition(actor) that resolves tier, coach_addons, EVA_DISABLED_MODULES kill-switch, and the body_composition cross-dependency in one place. Mandatory before exposing any billable integration endpoint (barcode API, food import, future wearable webhook) so a new surface cannot check one gate and leak the feature.

recommendations.title: Harden column grants on nutrition_plans and foods to match the clients doctrine
recommendations.detail: REVOKE UPDATE ON foods/nutrition_plans FROM authenticated, then GRANT UPDATE on a non-scope allowlist; make client_id/coach_id/org_id service-role-only. Prevents cross-tenant re-scoping and the coach_id->NULL global-pool promotion before an external catalog coexists with coach rows. Pair with a CI drift check against information_schema.column_privileges (mirror tests/separation/module-grants.sql).

recommendations.title: Model 3rd-party data licensing inside coach_addons and billing_snapshots
recommendations.detail: Add a data_source dimension/cost line so per-seat FatSecret (or equivalent) licensing maps onto entitlements and shows up in billing_snapshots evidence. Decide free-tracking / paid-advanced-planning split (industry-proven: Trainerize/Everfit gate generation, keep tracking free). This lets EVA charge for the nutrition module without absorbing untracked data-licensing cost.

recommendations.title: Wire an assessment -> TDEE engine that consumes the body_composition add-on
recommendations.detail: De-silo nutrition from body_composition: when ISAK/BIA lean mass exists, switch the energy equation from Mifflin-St Jeor to Cunningham/Katch-McArdle; add adaptive TDEE that reverse-calculates from logged intake + weight trend (converges 2-3 weeks) instead of freezing the signup value. Protein-forward defaults. Validate 4P+4C+9F approx kcal server-side as a safety rail.

recommendations.title: Add Apple Health / Garmin / Fitbit expenditure import as a P3 integration once intake exists
recommendations.detail: After the real-intake model lands, auto-import expenditure/sleep/RHR to feed adaptive TDEE, and Dexcom/Libre CGM for metabolic clients. Sequenced last because it is meaningless until intake-vs-target is real; build it on the unified entitlement resolver as a billable depth feature.

quickWins: REVOKE UPDATE on foods.coach_id from authenticated to kill the coach_id->NULL global-pool promotion vector (single migration).
Add a source_db + verified column to foods now (default coach_custom/false) so provenance exists before any catalog ingest, even before the ETL is built.
Wire the already-installed expo-camera to an Open Food Facts barcode lookup as a read-only food-search prefill (free API, no licensing) to prove the multi-modal path.
Relabel 'consumed' macros in the alumno/coach UI as 'plan adherence' to stop implying real intake tracking the model does not perform.
Surface the existing Edamam /coach/recipes data into the PlanBuilder food search instead of leaving it quarantined in a separate route.
